2026-06-02 (Drop 2 follow-up — respawn-grace consumer WIRED): Closed the flagged Drop-2 loose end. engine.death.get_respawn_grace_until (reader) + the grace_until writer in on_pc_death shipped in Drop 2, but the reader had ZERO callers — the anti-spawn-camp protection its docstring promised did nothing (infrastructure-complete-but-unwired; verified by grep). Wired the consumer into parser/combat_commands.py _apply_combat_wear's PC-death block: before on_pc_death finalizes a death, it now calls get_respawn_grace_until(ctx.db, c.id); if the victim is still inside their grace window, the killing blow is REFUSED — wound_level capped at INCAPACITATED (alive, downed; NOT MORTALLY_WOUNDED, so no bleed-out next round), re-synced to DB + session, player notified ([RESPAWN PROTECTION] ...), and on_pc_death (corpse / inventory drop / wound debuff / BH-insurance) skipped entirely. Otherwise the normal death path is unchanged. Reader fails OPEN to 0.0 so a read error never grants invulnerability; grace is only written on PvP deaths (killer_id present), so the window is a PvP-respawn protection (RESPAWN_GRACE_SECONDS = 60s); while protected, repeated blows just re-cap at INCAPACITATED until the window elapses. Scope = refuse the lethal blow during the window regardless of attacker type (simple, faithful to the docstring); can tighten to same-killer-only later. Tests: tests/test_drop2_respawn_grace_wiring.py (+8: 5 source-structure wiring pins [reader called, gated before on_pc_death, caps at INCAPACITATED, death path intact, no-throw-guarded] + 3 real-db reader-contract checks, skip-guarded on the db backend). Sandbox verification: 8 wiring + 16 Drop-2 reconciliation (no regression) + 113 death/combat (test_pg1_death_a/b, test_combat_mechanics, test_session54_combat_umbrella) all green after installing aiosqlite+bcrypt in the sandbox. PENDING on Brian's box: full ~4,854 suite; in-client PvP smoke (kill a PC, respawn, attempt re-kill within 60s -> blow turned aside, no second corpse). Note: combat death finalization is in parser/combat_commands.py _apply_combat_wear, NOT engine/combat.py (the earlier handoff said 'combat.py' loosely; the actual consumer site is the command layer where on_pc_death is gated).

2026-06-03 (Drop 3 B4 — gear insurance): Completed the policyholder side of insurance that Drop 2 death-reconciliation deferred. A one-shot policy: +insure buy debits a flat premium (GEAR_INSURANCE_PREMIUM=500, tunable) via adjust_credits('gear_insurance_premium', a sink); on the next lawless/contested death on_pc_death keeps the holder's loose loadout on them (instead of dropping to a re-lootable corpse) and consumes the policy; corpse still made but empty of their gear. KEY DESIGN CALL: RESTORATION model, NOT a credit indemnity — this death model sends gear to a re-lootable corpse, so a credit payout would be a suicide-faucet (die->re-loot->keep payout). No payout = nothing to farm = exploit-safe; premium is the only credit movement. Uninsured deaths still drop gear (sink survives). engine/gear_insurance.py (pure), parser/insurance_commands.py (+insure/buy/cancel, registered in game_server.py), db/database.py migration v39 (characters.gear_insured + allowlist; SCHEMA_VERSION 39), engine/death.py on_pc_death protection branch (fail-open). +16 tests (test_gear_insurance.py); Drop 2 death (16) + ledger pin (4) green. Refund-safe. Surfaces on @economy automatically. TUNABLE: flat premium for v1 — if everyone insures (eroding the gear-loss sink), raise it or scale by loadout value (valuation affects only fairness, never exploit-safety). SCHEMA NOTE: v39 — if a concurrent drop also claims v39, renumber. PENDING Brian: full Windows suite + smoke (+insure buy charges 500; lawless death keeps gear + consumes policy vs uninsured drops; secured no-op). Remaining Drop 3: B2 home/city prestige, B3 vanity/regalia, A3 spy intel-desk, A5 Hutt den, A8 entertainer audience-weighting, faction mission configs/commissary (A4).

2026-06-05 (Test-infra fix — run_all_tests.bat 'ERROR: usage: ... [file_or_dir]', reported from a Windows run): The runner unconditionally passed --timeout=120 --timeout-method=thread; those flags only exist when pytest-timeout is installed, and Brian's env didn't have it, so pytest rejected the whole invocation with a usage/unrecognized-arguments error and wrote no useful log (findstr then surfaced the 'ERROR: usage:' line). Reproduced the exact error in a clean env (pytest WITHOUT pytest-timeout). FIX: run_all_tests.bat now probes `python -c "import pytest_timeout"` and only adds the --timeout flags when it imports (errorlevel 0); otherwise it prints a one-line `pip install pytest-timeout` hint and runs WITHOUT the per-test timeout, so the suite runs either way and writes tests_output.log. Verified both branches in a clean env (absent -> flags dropped, 1 passed; present -> flags accepted, 1 passed). -o addopts= already neutralizes pytest.ini so the ini wasn't involved. run_regression_tests.bat does NOT pass --timeout (not affected; still uses the older Tee-Object redirection — separate lower-priority item, left unchanged). RECOMMEND Brian: pip install pytest-timeout (or -r requirements.txt) to restore hang-protection. Handoff: HANDOFF_run_all_tests_fix_2026-06-05.md.

Killing a wilderness-encounter creature lets the killer field-dress it: a Survival check (data-driven DC) yields a resource stack (organic hides/pelts; chemical for the crab water-sacs + DC-10 spor venom) into inventory.resources via crafting.add_resource. 7 creatures gained wired harvest blocks (hitcher_crab, magus, stalker_lizard, voroos[yield 2], wrix, tymp, spor_crawler[DC 10]); both live biomes (Dune Sea + Coruscant Underworld) balanced organic+chemical. ECONOMY ANALYSIS (Brian flagged flood risk post-audit): traced resource->value plumbing — raw resources have NO NPC buyer (the `sell` builtin takes equipped ITEMS only and refuses good crafts per econ v2 §1.3); resources monetize only P2P via escrow-backed vendor-droid buy orders (vendor_buy_order_escrow — money supply untouched) or are consumed by crafting. => spoils mint ZERO credits, NO inflation vector. tier_1_active.delivered_collect: 2026-06-06 — the hunter's PC-death COLLECT-CONSEQUENCE (formerly the last deferred piece; the inverse of on_dsp_hunter_killed). engine/dsp_hunter.py: new pure decider hunter_collected_line (the hunter DOWNS its quarry — distinct from the escape-path collected_line, which the tick's escape-reconcile still uses). engine/dsp_hunter_runtime.py: on_quarry_collected(db, victim_id, session_mgr=, room_id=) — cheap no-op when no live hunter is attached; when one is, it announces the collect beat to the room, despawns the hunter NPC (contract fulfilled), and CLEARS the pursuit so it resets to a FRESH start (NOT the escape-reconcile 'imminent' reset — the quarry was collected, not slipped; a new pursuit rebuilds from progress 0 since the PC is still on the dark path). engine/death.py: on_pc_death gains an optional session_mgr kwarg and fires the hook at the TOP of the body (before the secured-zone NO_CORPSE early return) so it triggers on EVERY death path; parser/combat_commands.py passes ctx.session_mgr at the call site (the only real caller). Prestige-domain: the quarry's standing death penalty (Wounded + corpse + insurance) already applies; the collect takes no credits and changes no DSP — fixing the prior gap where a hunter that killed its quarry lingered until the next tick and the escape-reconcile mis-reset it to 'imminent'. Tests: tests/test_hunter_collect_consequence.py (+6: pure kill-line distinct/clean/named; runtime no-op-without-pursuit, no-op-without-live-hunter, despawn+clear+announce, room-resolved-from-NPC; on_pc_death fires-the-hook via the secured-zone path with the hook monkeypatched). 6/6 green; the on_pc_death optional-kwarg add left every death-path suite green (gear_insurance / pg1_death_a engine-consumers / pg1_death_b / drop2 reconciliation+grace).

Its findings were remediated across the economy-hardening + audit-v2 wave (Drops 1.a-1.c ledger chokepoint, the audit-v2 9-item remediation incl. market_state pool persistence + F2 yard-repair sink + NPC craft-buyback refusal, Drop 3 willing sinks, Drop 4 social-Force economy) - see CHANGELOG 2026-06-02..04 and arch v51 §1.4-G. RESIDUAL (post-launch, NOT 'review undone'): the audit's own Phase-3 triage-deferred items (#12 dynamic supply/demand curves, #13-#17 = Month-1+ by the economist's own call) and the tunable_open_questions numeric knobs (now owned by T3.19 telemetry-grounded tuning, not by an unrun review).

tier_2_queued.engine_state: Admin-grant works today — admins can manually add the schematic key to a character's attributes.schematics list. No story-line surface for non-admin acquisition.

The crafted ItemInstance rows land in inventory correctly, but the items don't yet do anything in combat/space. Also needs an item-mod-to-ship application surface — likely a parser command like 'install <item>' or admin-applies-to-ship.

tier_2_queued.title: T5 schematic discoverability UI in 'craft' command
tier_2_queued.design_doc: Existing parser/crafting_commands.py — extend the 'craft' command's schematic-list output to surface T5 entries with appropriate gating-info display (which T5 mat needed, current quality available in inventory, can_craft yes/no).
tier_2_queued.scope: Today the 'craft' command lists all schematics in a character's attributes.schematics. T5 schematics work for any character who has them in that list (via admin-grant or the future T2.DEF.t5_trainer_storyline path). The discoverability concern is: even with a schematic granted, the player doesn't see a clear 'you have q72 kyber, need q75 to craft this' diagnostic. Surface that.
tier_2_queued.closed_by: Crafting command repair + T5 discoverability drop
tier_2_queued.resolution_note: Closed by the 2026-05-31 crafting-command repair drop. The discoverability ask itself was Bug A: engine.crafting.check_resources now distinguishes QUANTITY-blocked from QUALITY-blocked failures, so a crafter holding 5x q72 kyber is told the grade is short (best q72, need q75+) instead of the misleading 'need 3x more'. While in the file, four adjacent schema-drift bugs in parser/crafting_commands.py were fixed: (B1) `schematics` listing read resource_requirements+skill -> 'Needs: none' for all 38 recipes, now reads components+skill_required with a [*] craftable flag; (B2) `resources` listing read r['amount'] -> KeyError crash, now reads quantity; (C) `craft` rolled a non-existent 'repair' skill, now rolls skill_required; (D, SEVERE) `craft` passed a float to resolve_craft -> AttributeError on every craft with materials in hand (command was non-functional end-to-end), now passes the SkillCheckResult. +13 tests (test_crafting_discoverability.py); 488 neighbor green.

tier_2_queued.title: Coruscant Underworld wilderness build
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped as single-level region per Brian's call to collapse the design's mid/low/bottom Z-axis. Loader extended with generic landmark_includes: mechanism (W.3); Dune Sea migrated to the same mechanism for parity. 5 landmarks + 3 transit nodes load cleanly. era.yaml wired. 24 new tests + 153 existing wilderness tests green. Encounter/hazard system explicitly deferred to T2.W.encounters (covers both regions).

tier_2_queued.build_out_2026_06_12: Drop 18 (handoff item 4): built out beyond the 8 design-doc anchors with 12 secondary landmarks across the 4 quadrants (NW industrial overflow / NE smuggler reaches / SW deep warren / SE Maze fringe), one disjoint-quadrant include file each, coordinate-placed (no adjacency) per the anchor pattern. Region now loads 20 landmarks, 0 errors/warnings, unique coords+ids. CONSUMED today: each landmark = a reachable described room (name/description/short_desc/terrain/wilderness_landmark) via wilderness_writer. FORWARD-LOOKING (pre-existing, affects the 8 anchors identically): ambient_lines ride TD.WILDERNESS_AMBIENT_LINES_DEAD_WRITE (no runtime reader yet); gameplay props (threat_tier/gameplay_role/faction_anchor) await the encounter-spawner phase; the wilderness MAP renderer is still hardcoded to Dune Sea (design §4a generalization — JS/SPA, Brian-Windows/browser pass) so the region renders as sand until §4a ships. Pin test tests/test_coruscant_underworld_buildout.py.

tier_2_queued.title: Pre-flight + Migration Plan for Contestable Wilderness pivot
tier_2_queued.priority_note: FIRST in SYN sequence. No engine code ships in SYN.0; verification + plan + migration skeleton.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Pre-flight audit conducted, migration script skeleton authored at tools/syn_migration.py, deprecated surfaces tagged with docstring headers, TODO.json::tech_debt surface list corrected. Shipped in the BugFix5_plus_SYN0 combined drop alongside five test-only bugfixes.
pre_flight_findings: Finding 1: TODO.json::tech_debt named MAX_CLAIMS_PER_ZONE / MAX_CLAIMS_PER_ORG; actual constants in engine/territory.py:66-67 are CLAIM_MAX_PER_ZONE = 3 and CLAIM_MAX_TOTAL = 10. The surface list is corrected in this drop.

Finding 2: is_room_claimed_by has 6 consumers beyond engine/security.py::_apply_claim_upgrade: parser/faction_commands.py (armory gate, 2 sites), engine/territory.py (armory storage/withdraw, 3 internal sites), engine/sleeping.py (sleeping bonus, 1 site). Design doc §3.1 only named _apply_claim_upgrade. SYN.1 must retarget or retire each of these 6 sites; the design doc estimate of ~1 sess still holds since each site is mechanical (region-scope swap) but the call-site count is recorded here so SYN.1's pre-flight doesn't re-discover it.

Finding 3: engine/player_cities.py::found_city is NOT anchor-agnostic at HEAD. Steps 7-10 of the validation chain hard-code the org's tier-5 HQ entry room → that room's zone_id → that zone's declared_security → org influence in that zone. SYN.4 rewrites all four steps to anchor on a wilderness region. The design doc's ~2 sess estimate for SYN.4 is consistent with this scope.

Finding 4: engine/player_cities.py::claim_room_for_city (city expansion) was added later than the design doc accounted for. SYN.4 retires this entirely — the per-room expansion model goes away when cities anchor on regions; expansion happens via wilderness landmark capture + building construction (SYN.9).

SYN.1 unsplit would land ~1500 LOC across schema + new functions + tick retargets + garrison spawn + six cross-module call-site retargets + ~30 tests in one drop. Splitting halves the wedge risk and gives a chance to validate the new API shape before it goes load-bearing.

tier_2_queued.resolution: Stub-and-defang strategy adopted instead of physical deletion (wider blast radius than SYN.0 Finding 2 anticipated: get_claim/get_claim_display_tag/get_claims_status_lines/_GUARD_TEMPLATES have fan-in from session.py HUD, builtin_commands.py look output, combat_commands.py hostile takeover (SYN.3's domain), and the faction _cmd_guard handler — physical deletion would have required simultaneous edits to ~7 modules). The 8 legacy surfaces are stubbed to no-ops with RETIRED docstrings: claim_room/unclaim_room return ok=False with deprecation messages; is_room_claimed_by always returns False; spawn_guard_npc/remove_guard_npc return ok=False (remove also accepts **kwargs to tolerate the housing.py force=True call that was always silently failing); tick_claim_maintenance and tick_resource_nodes are no-ops; _apply_claim_upgrade (pre-empted from SYN.2) returns base unchanged. CLAIM_MAX_PER_ZONE and CLAIM_MAX_TOTAL physically deleted (only used inside claim_room). get_claims_status_lines retargeted to iterate region_ownership. The six is_room_claimed_by callers + the two faction parser commands + the two tick handlers all retargeted to the SYN.1.a region surfaces. wipe_territory_claims wired into ensure_territory_schema via _syn1b_wipe_territory_claims_once with a syn_migration_state marker table for idempotency. One existing test (TestClaimUpgradeCompose in test_secmod1_admin_security.py) updated to reflect the no-op _apply_claim_upgrade.
tier_2_queued.preempted_from_syn2: engine/security.py::_apply_claim_upgrade — stubbed to no-op in SYN.1.b because it cannot function without is_room_claimed_by. SYN.2's remaining scope (wilderness-aware security branch in get_effective_security) is unaffected.

tier_2_queued.title: Wilderness-aware security
tier_2_queued.scope: engine/security.py step 4 (wilderness branch, terminal for wilderness chars); _get_wilderness_region_state + _apply_wilderness_ownership helpers; _apply_claim_upgrade deletes.
tier_2_queued.note_preempted_in_syn1b: _apply_claim_upgrade was stubbed to no-op in SYN.1.b (could not function without is_room_claimed_by). SYN.2's remaining work: ship the wilderness-aware security branch in get_effective_security (step 4) — read region_ownership directly when room.wilderness_region_id is set; physically delete the _apply_claim_upgrade stub from _finalize after the new branch ships.
tier_2_queued.resolution: New wilderness-aware step 4 added to engine/security.py::get_effective_security per design §2.3. Two new helpers: _get_wilderness_region_state(room, db) reads default_security from the wilderness_regions registry + owner from region_ownership (SYN.1.a); _apply_wilderness_ownership(base, char, region_state) is the pure citadel-upgrade rule (LAWLESS → CONTESTED for owners, base stands for outsiders or unowned regions, CONTESTED stays CONTESTED — no double promotion). Step 4 is terminal for wilderness rooms (skips steps 5-6 room-prop fallback). _apply_claim_upgrade physically deleted; _finalize stripped of its claim_upgrade call. Director overlays (steps 1-3) still short-circuit step 4 — admin overrides win. City-map resolution path unchanged. Pre-flight confirmed wilderness_regions table is populated by engine/wilderness_writer.py at world-build time and read elsewhere in production (parser/builtin_commands.py wilderness travel commands); rooms in unregistered regions fall through gracefully to step 5. Adjacent regression: 313 tests green (SYN.1.a region ownership, SYN.2 wilderness branch, secmod1 admin security, territory constants, wenc, security resolver runtime + writer merge + zone coverage + yaml audit, wilderness drop2 + drop2_phase2, TODO/CHANGELOG hygiene); cities phases 1-7c 396 tests green; combat 77 tests green.

tier_2_queued.title: Active harvest mechanic (first half of SYN.6)
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped engine/harvest.py (~510 LOC) + parser/harvest_command.py + 57 tests. Pure helpers (_yield_table_lookup, _apply_skill_margin, _quality_to_resource_quality, _compute_tax, compute_harvest_payout) + DB-touching perform_harvest. 6-row yield table per design §2.5.2 verbatim (security × influence-tier). Survival skill check at DC 6 (WEG Easy). 30-min per-region cooldown with per-region namespacing. 15% non-owner credit tax routed to owner treasury via existing adjust_org_treasury seam. Resource stacks land in inventory.resources via engine.crafting.add_resource — same storage crafting already reads from, no schema migration. Region-quality multiplier is a seam (_get_region_quality) returning 1.0 in SYN.6.a; SYN.6.b is a one-function swap. T5 rare hits grant +1 'rare' stack at q100 (top of band) so existing crafting sees them immediately and T5 recipes can gate on min_quality ≥ 95 when they land. Influence: NONE — pinned by TestPerformHarvestNoInfluence per §4.25 wilderness-only-influence invariant + design §2.7 reward table. 10/10 deterministic runs after fixing the test fixture's skill field placement (skills are a top-level char dict field, not nested in attributes).

tier_2_queued.title: Weekly region-quality variance + Director resource-outlook digest (second half of SYN.6)
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped engine/region_quality.py (~290 LOC) — full weekly-quality subsystem: schema (region_quality table, composite PK region_slug+resource_type), pure helpers (_iso_year_week canonical 'YYYY-Www', _compute_weekly_multiplier 0.7..1.3 with 2-decimal rounding, _outlook_summary best/worst with stable tie-breaking), DB-touching wrappers (roll_region_quality idempotent per ISO week, get_region_quality_for fail-soft dict[type,float], get_outlook with optional org filter, tick_weekly_region_quality iterating SELECT DISTINCT wilderness_region_id FROM rooms — broader than region_ownership so un-owned regions get rolls too since SYN.6.a maps them to fallback yields). Schema bootstrap is per-feature (ensure_region_quality_schema) not a SCHEMA_VERSION bump — mirrors SYN.1.a pattern. compute_harvest_payout in engine/harvest.py now polymorphic on quality: accepts float (SYN.6.a back-compat) OR dict[type, float] (SYN.6.b consumer); new stack_qualities result field. _get_region_quality seam swapped from return 1.0 to delegating into engine.region_quality.get_region_quality_for with fail-soft fallback. parser/faction_commands.py new _cmd_resource_outlook subcommand (aliases: outlook, resources) with green/yellow/dim color-grading by best-multiplier. Hourly tick (region_quality_weekly_tick) with per-region per-week ISO 8601 anchor — matches city_maintenance_tick pattern, simpler than wiring cron on interval-based scheduler. 38 new tests across 10 sections, 95 tests across SYN.6.a+SYN.6.b combined, 5/5 deterministic runs. Adjacent SYN family (394) + faction tests (94) all green.

tier_2_queued.title: Wilderness anomalies Tier 1 (CW-correct, skill-check resolution)
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped engine/wilderness_anomalies.py (~600 LOC) with substrate + Tier 1 templates + skill-check resolution. Module-level transient state keyed by region_slug (restart wipes, matches engine.space_anomalies pattern). 5 CW-correct templates: stranded_clone_scout (Republic, Medicine/Survival), salvage_cache (Technical/Survival), wounded_animal (Survival/Brawling), tusken_party (Blaster/Melee), crashed_cis_probe (Technical/Knowledge). All templates ship simplified combat-resolution via skill check rather than full NPC spawn — proper NPC spawn for templates 3-4 (wounded_animal, tusken_party) defers to a post-launch polish drop. parser/anomaly_commands.py NEW: AnomaliesCommand (lists active anomalies in caller's wilderness region with ~Nm-left countdown) + InvestigateCommand (resolves anomaly with skill check). server/tick_handlers_economy.py: wilderness_anomaly_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: scheduler registration (interval=3600, offset=1500). Hourly cadence + 0.4 spawn-chance per region per tick → expected ~2.5h avg interval per region (matches design 'every 2-3 hours per region'). MAX_PER_REGION=2 cap. TIER1_DURATION_SECS=1800 (30 min). +5 influence to resolver's faction on success. News broadcast on each spawn via session_mgr.broadcast (best-effort). One-shot resolution semantics: failed skill check still consumes the anomaly and grants reduced fail_reward (anti-farm + low frustration). 41 new tests across 10 sections, 5/5 deterministic. Adjacent SYN family (570) + new = 611 tests green.

tier_2_queued.title: SYN.7.a Coruscant gap + real NPC combat (corner-cut closures)
tier_2_queued.design_doc: Implicit in SYN.7.a + Brian's directive 2026-05-25: 'complete SYN' means actually complete, not corner-cut. Two gaps closed: (1) SYN.7.a templates were all Dune Sea-flavored; the Coruscant Underworld wilderness region (real and active) had zero anomaly coverage. (2) wounded_animal + tusken_party resolved as skill check vs DC 13 rather than real combat encounters.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Rewrote engine/wilderness_anomalies.py to ~1100 LOC. 10 templates now: 5 Dune Sea (unchanged keys; wounded_animal + tusken_party flipped skill → combat) + 5 new Coruscant Underworld (black_sun_courier, factory_cache, maze_rogue, cis_sleeper_cell, bounty_hunter_rival). New per-template fields: 'regions: [...]' (region-tag list; REGION_ANY sentinel for universal) and 'resolution: skill|combat'. _pick_template now filters by region; returns None for unknown regions (system stays silent in un-templated regions rather than spawning random content). Combat-mode templates declare 'combat_npcs: [...]' specs (archetype + tier + species + name_pool + weapon + behavior + personality) — one real NPC spawned per spec via db.create_npc; ai_config_json tagged with is_anomaly_target + anomaly_id. New helpers: award_combat_anomaly_reward (the kill-hook entry point; decrements live-NPC list, fires payout on last-hostile death), find_anomaly_globally (cross-region anomaly lookup for the kill hook), _prune_expired_region_with_cleanup (deletes surviving NPCs from expired combat anomalies), _gate_investigate + _resolve_anomaly_skill + _resolve_anomaly_combat (split resolution paths). parser/combat_commands.py: anomaly kill hook inserted between bounty kill hook and WoW.3a kill credit; mirrors bounty pattern (reads ai_config_json.is_anomaly_target on dying NPC, calls award_combat_anomaly_reward, surfaces [ANOMALY CLEARED] line via session_mgr.find_by_character; attribution via c.last_attacker_id, same chain as bounty + WoW.3a). parser/anomaly_commands.py: InvestigateCommand branches on result.mode — combat-mode shows long_desc + 'use attack' nudge; skill-mode shows the roll + verdict + resource grants. Resolution mix: 4 skill (stranded_clone_scout, salvage_cache, crashed_cis_probe, factory_cache) + 6 combat. CW-grep extended to Coruscant templates and verified. 26 new tests (41 → 67 in test_syn7a_wilderness_anomalies.py, 5/5 deterministic). _MiniDB extended with npcs table + create_npc/get_npc/delete_npc/update_npc/get_npcs_in_room for combat-path tests. Adjacent SYN family (501 tests across SYN.1.a..SYN.7.a): zero regressions. Combat-adjacent files (147 tests across wow3a/wow3b/pvp_flag/sess54_combat_umbrella/sess58_cleanup/sess63_bulk_premium): zero regressions.

tier_2_queued.title: Wilderness anomalies Tier 2 (CW-correct, multi-phase combat)
tier_2_queued.scope: 5 CW-correct Tier 2 templates (3 Dune Sea + 2 Coruscant; region parity from SYN.7.a.fix): downed_republic_acclamator (3 phases — CIS scout element → heavy response → salvage commander; T5 drops weapons_capacitor_core), hutt_smuggling_convoy (2 phases — outriders → convoy heavies; T5 drops weapons_capacitor_core), cis_commando_deployment (3 phases — BX vanguard → heavy support → tactical droid commander; named item loot: T-Series Tactical Droid Command Module), maze_predator_outbreak (3 phases — scout pack → main pack → alpha; T5 drops composite_chitin), coruscant_gang_war (2 phases — first volley → boss engagement; named item loot: Black Sun Vigo Signet Ring). Cadence: 6h tick × 0.20 chance → ~30h avg interval. Per-region cap 1 (T2 is the headline event). 2h duration. +20 influence (mid of design's 15-25 band).
tier_2_queued.resolution: Extended engine/wilderness_anomalies.py from ~1100 LOC to ~1500 LOC. New TIER2_TEMPLATES catalog with 5 templates, each with `phases: [...]` + optional `named_loot: {...}`. Multi-phase combat dispatch: investigate spawns ONLY phase 0 NPCs; killing the last NPC of phase N triggers _advance_to_next_phase which spawns phase N+1's NPCs (or fires final payout if at last phase). New helpers: _spawn_combat_npcs (extracted shared spawn loop), _advance_to_next_phase (phase advancement with intro broadcast), _payout_combat_anomaly (split T1 single-char from T2 multi-char payout), _grant_named_loot (resource + item shapes). Tier 2 payout distributes credits/resources to ALL characters in anchor room (split equally for credits; resource list shared); killer alone gets influence (+20 to faction) and named loot. WildernessAnomaly dataclass extended with tier (default 1), current_phase, total_phases property, is_final_phase property. _pick_template signature gained tier=int parameter (1 or 2); selects from TIER1_TEMPLATES vs TIER2_TEMPLATES disjointly. spawn_anomaly_for_region gained tier kwarg with per-tier duration + cap. New tick: tick_tier2_wilderness_anomalies (separate from Tier 1 tick — caps, durations, chances all independent). parser/combat_commands.py kill hook extended: detects tier from payout, surfaces per-participant [ANOMALY CLEARED] lines for Tier 2; killer gets +inf line + [NAMED LOOT] line; passes session_mgr through to award_combat_anomaly_reward for phase-intro broadcasts. server/tick_handlers_economy.py: tier2_wilderness_anomaly_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: scheduler registration (interval=21600, offset=3300 — won't collide with Tier 1's offset=1500).
files_touched: engine/wilderness_anomalies.py (modified — added ~400 LOC: TIER2_TEMPLATES catalog, Tier 2 constants, multi-phase resolution helpers, split payout function, named-loot grant function)
parser/combat_commands.py (modified — anomaly kill hook extended for Tier 2 multi-participant payout shape + named-loot line; passes session_mgr through)
closes: T2.DEF.t5_drop_hooks.composite_chitin (partially closed — Maze outbreak named-loot drop; T3 Apex participation-scaled drop still queued for SYN.8)

tier_2_queued.title: Wilderness anomalies Tier 3 (world bosses)
tier_2_queued.scope: 4 CW-correct Tier 3 templates per design verbatim: krayt_dragon (Dune Sea, 3 phases — juvenile pack guards → elder krayt → enraged krayt; trophy: Krayt Dragon Scale; scaled T5 mat: deep_dune_iron q80), maze_predator_apex (Coruscant Underworld, 3 phases — pack vanguard → the apex → frenzy; trophy: Maze Apex Fang; scaled T5 mat: composite_chitin q80), crashed_separatist_capital_ship (REGION_ANY, 3 phases — perimeter security → heavy response → tactical command; trophy: Salvaged Separatist Hull Plate; scaled T5 mat: weapons_capacitor_core q80), republic_lost_patrol (REGION_ANY, 3 phases — outer picket → interrogation detail → final reinforcements; trophy: Republic Patrol Insignia; scaled T5 mat: weapons_capacitor_core q80). Cadence: daily tick × 0.10 chance → ~10-day avg per region (mid of design's 7-14d). Per-region cap 1. 8h duration. +50 influence to killing-blow faction.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Extended engine/wilderness_anomalies.py from ~1500 LOC to ~2000 LOC. New TIER3_TEMPLATES catalog with 4 templates, each adding trophy_per_participant + scaled_t5_mat fields. Tier 3 reuses Tier 2's multi-phase machinery (investigate spawns phase 0; phase advancement on phase clear; final clear triggers payout). WildernessAnomaly extended with kill_counts: dict[char_id, int] tracked by the kill hook. _resolve_anomaly_combat + _advance_to_next_phase + award_combat_anomaly_reward all changed `tier == 2` to `tier >= 2` so the same machinery handles T3. _payout_combat_anomaly rewritten as tier dispatch: T1 = single-char (unchanged), T2 = room-occupant enumeration (unchanged), T3 = kill_counts.keys() as participants (anyone who killed any anomaly NPC during the encounter). New helpers: _grant_trophy (adds item to inventory['items'] with is_trophy:True + is_anomaly_loot:True), _distribute_scaled_t5_mat (floor(N/4) pieces ranked by kill count descending; killer wins ties; minimum 1 piece consolation for small teams). New tick_tier3_wilderness_anomalies parallel to T1/T2 ticks. parser/combat_commands.py kill hook extended: detects tier=3; surfaces [WORLD BOSS DEFEATED] headline, [TROPHY] line per participant, [T5 MATERIAL] line for top scaled-T5 recipients with kill count. server/tick_handlers_economy.py: tier3_wilderness_anomaly_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: scheduler registration (interval=86400, offset=7200 — won't collide with T1 offset=1500 or T2 offset=3300). Also fixed: total_phases property previously checked tier==2; now tier>=2.

tier_2_queued.title: Player-constructed building system
tier_2_queued.scope: engine/buildings.py (~1150 LOC) with 5 categories per design literal: residence (5000cr + 5 metal + 5 organic; 50-item personal storage), crafting_station (8000cr + 10 metal + 5 composite; +1D crafting bonus lookup), commerce_stall (6000cr + 8 metal; 50/50 owner/city vendor split lookup), garrison_annex (10000cr + 15 metal + 5 chemical; spawns 2 defending NPCs at completion), cultural_hall (7500cr + 8 metal + 5 organic; +1 daily CP lookup). Construction takes 24 real-time hours; demolish refunds 25% materials; same-owner rebuild gets 10% discount. Mayor evict has 2-day notice. Slot capacity per landmark defaults to 2; force-resonant landmarks have 0 slots.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Shipped engine/buildings.py with full substrate: ensure_schema (idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), construct_building (validates rank 3+ in city's owning org + slot free + materials + credits; deducts on start; creates row with status='under_construction' + completion_ts=now+24h), demolish_building (owner-initiated; 25% refund if operational; no refund if under_construction; cleans up garrison NPCs), evict_building (mayor-initiated; sets evict_after_ts=now+2d; double-evict rejected), tick_building_construction (transitions under_construction→operational at 24h with garrison NPC spawn; transitions operational+evict-notice→evicted at 2-day expiry with NPC cleanup), 4 effect-lookup helpers (get_crafting_station_bonus, get_cultural_hall_in_room, get_commerce_stall_in_room, get_residence_for_owner), residence_store_item + residence_take_item (owner-only 50-item cap). parser/player_building_commands.py with PlayerBuildingCommand dispatch (7 subcommands: construct, demolish, evict, list, inspect, store, take). Filename chosen to avoid collision with existing parser/building_commands.py (admin world-building, @dig/@tunnel/etc — unrelated system). server/tick_handlers_economy.py: building_construction_tick wrapper. server/game_server.py: import + ensure_schema at boot + scheduler register (interval=300s = 5min, offset=120) + register_player_building_commands at parser bootstrap.
files_touched: engine/buildings.py (new — ~1150 LOC: schema, 5 categories, construct/demolish/evict, residence storage, garrison NPC spawn, tick, 4 effect-lookup helpers, rebuild discount)
server/game_server.py (modified — import + ensure_schema at boot + scheduler register + parser register)
tests/test_syn9_player_buildings.py (new — ~1100 LOC, 54 tests across 11 sections, 5/5 deterministic)
depends_on: engine.player_cities (DONE — get_city_for_room used to validate which city owns a landmark)
engine.organizations (DONE — get_membership used for rank check)
engine.npc_generator + ai.npc_brain (DONE — garrison NPC spawn at completion)
what_deferred_within_scope: Consumer integration of effect-lookup helpers. The substrate ships the 4 lookup functions but does NOT wire them into consumers — engine.crafting still does not consult get_crafting_station_bonus during craft rolls, engine.cp_engine still does not consult get_cultural_hall_in_room during daily CP tick, no vendor-surface command consumes get_commerce_stall_in_room. Lookup helpers ready for SYN.10 polish or post-launch wiring. This is the 'seam discipline' pattern (architecture v50): ship the contract first with no consumer, fail-loud on missing consumer rather than silent fallback.

Commerce stall full vendor surface (player-vendor commands for listing items, accepting buyer credits, 50/50 split with city treasury). Substrate detects the commerce_stall building exists in a room; the vendor command surface (analogous to bounty board or sabacc) is deferred.
Cultural hall time-tracking. The design says '5+ minutes here'; tracking per-char time-in-room requires either a periodic tick poll or hooking room-move events. Deferred.
Donate-to-org flow. construct_building has a `donate_to_org` parameter that sets owning_org_id on the building row; the parser surface doesn't expose it yet. Substrate supports it.
Building HP / combat damage. The schema has an hp column; nothing decrements it. Tier 3 anomaly events / contest damage as design mentions for destruction is deferred.
supersedes: T2.BUILD.* (player building plans rolled into this drop)

tier_2_queued.title: Display integration + launch polish
tier_2_queued.priority_note: FINAL in SYN sequence. Architecture v49 rollup opens next. T2.ECON.review opens.
tier_2_queued.scope: Display integration: engine/territory_display.py (new, ~890 LOC) with web-UI-contract data dict (get_region_data_block) + ANSI CLI renderer (get_region_look_block) + faction-scoped renderers (get_faction_contests_lines/data, get_faction_resource_outlook_lines/data) + 6 news-format helpers (ownership_change, contest_start, contest_resolve, anomaly_defeat, building_completion, building_demolition). parser/region_commands.py (new) — +region [slug] command. Region info block auto-injects into wilderness `look` between security/movement and adjacent terrain. +faction contest + +faction resource_outlook subcommands wired into existing FactionUmbrellaCommand. News broadcast hooks added at: anomaly defeat (T1/T2/T3 payout), claim_region/unclaim_region return-dict `news` field, garrison_annex completion (global broadcast; other building categories stay owner-only). Contest start/resolve broadcasts predated SYN.10 with [REGION CONTEST]/[REGION DEFENDED]/[REGION SEIZED] tags — design-equivalent, not retouched.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Substantial UI-pivot-bridge content shipped. Data contract (get_region_data_block) is the canonical web-UI surface. ANSI palette centralized in engine/territory_display.py constants (_RED/_YELLOW/_GREEN/_CYAN/_MAGENTA/_BOLD/_DIM/_ITALIC) — web theme maps these to semantic CSS classes. All *_lines functions accept ansi=False for plain-text rendering (test snapshots, log dumps, structured data consumers). Failure-tolerant by design: any sub-section failure (missing wilderness_regions row, missing region_quality data, no contest active) silently no-ops so the render never breaks the wilderness look itself.

tier_2_queued.title: Security Model v1 content reconciliation
tier_2_queued.status: DONE (2026-06-05) — Coruscant migrated to §3 function-based zones + §3 security
tier_2_queued.why: YAML edit per §3 catalog. Live Coruscant uses tier-based names; catalog expects function-based names.
tier_2_queued.decision: Brian 2026-06-05: 'utility in A'. Adopt the §3 function-based Coruscant zone taxonomy.
tier_2_queued.resolution: Brian delegated the call (use judgment, complete implementation). Migrated all 8 Coruscant vertical-tier zones to the §3.1 function taxonomy: coruscant_senate→senate_district, coruscant_temple→jedi_temple, coruscant_upper→monumental_district, coruscant_midlevels→commercial_district, coruscant_works→commercial_district (MERGED — §3 mandates exactly one contested hand-built zone, so no off-catalog industrial zone; the Works is CSF-patrolled infrastructure = Secured/commercial), coruscant_gilded_cage→entertainment_district, coruscant_lower→southern_underground (the single Contested hand-built zone, the Cantina-Sanctuary archetype), coruscant_underworld unchanged (Lawless). Applied §3 security across ALL THREE schemes that had drifted apart: zones.yaml properties.security (canonical, read by security.py), coruscant.yaml per-room security_level (27 secured/13 contested/1 lawless), and the hardcoded _ZONE_SECURITY dict in engine/housing_lots_provider.py. Renamed across 32 files + zones.yaml keys + the npcs_drop_c2 roster file (coruscant_lower→southern_underground, on-disk rename). validate_world: 0 errors; 72/72 unique room slugs (merge created no collisions); no leftover old refs. Guard: tests/test_t2_5_and_t2_11b_morale_and_rezone.py. NOTE for Windows: the old roster file npcs_drop_c2_coruscant_lower.yaml must be removed (Expand-Archive can't delete a renamed file).

tier_2_queued.title: E3 (DESIGN): the remaining world-event FLAG effects — new player interactions (contraband_scan WIRED 2026-06-06; 5 remain: rare_vendor, hutt_auction, krayt_bounty, brawl_active, distress_active)

UPDATE 2026-06-06: contraband_scan is DONE — it needed NO design pass because it fit the existing smuggling lockdown precedent (a SECURITY_CHECKPOINT raises smuggling-run patrol scrutiny: +CHECKPOINT_PATROL_BOOST 0.15 chance / +CHECKPOINT_DIFFICULTY_BOOST 3 difficulty in engine/smuggling.py::resolve_patrol_encounter, sourced via get_effect at both smuggling_commands call sites, stacking with lockdown). No new command, faucet-safe (a caught smuggler forfeits per the existing path). bounty_reward_mult is also already live (E2). The 5 remaining FLAG effects (rare_vendor, hutt_auction+criminal_rep_gate, krayt_bounty, brawl_active, distress_active) DO still need a design call — each enables a genuinely new interaction (special vendor stock / auction / hunt / joinable brawl / distress encounter) with no existing mechanic to extend.
tier_2_queued.context: E2 wired the passive world-event multipliers. The remaining 6 effects are FLAGS that each ENABLE a NEW player interaction and need a design call before any code: (1) contraband_scan (SECURITY_CHECKPOINT) — sale-time scan RISK when selling contraband: what is the consequence (forfeit goods? fine? arrest chance?) and at which sale site? (2) rare_vendor (MERCHANT_ARRIVAL) — a vendor offers RARE stock while active: which items, from which pool, at what price, on which vendor surface? (3) hutt_auction + criminal_rep_gate:30 (HUTT_AUCTION) — an AUCTION interaction gated on criminal reputation >= 30: new command? what's auctioned, bidding model, where does rep come from? (4) krayt_bounty (KRAYT_SIGHTING) — a special high-value BOUNTY/hunt becomes available: bounty-board hook or new target spawn? (5) brawl_active (CANTINA_BRAWL) — a joinable cantina BRAWL: join command, resolution (skill check?), reward/risk? (6) distress_active (DISTRESS_SIGNAL) — an answerable DISTRESS beacon: accept command, what's the encounter/reward? Each should reuse existing systems where possible (bounty board, vendor-droid market, reputation, encounter pipeline) rather than bespoke mechanics.
tier_2_queued.blocks_or_after: after E2 (done); design-only first, then implement. Some overlap with sourcebook enrichment (e.g. krayt_bounty <-> creature/bounty content; hutt_auction <-> GG11 criminal-org content).

tier_2_queued.title: Web UI expansion package FOR Claude Design — bring gameplay surfaces out of the text-only interface (Brian, 2026-06-06)

tier_2_queued.status: IMPLEMENTATION IN PROGRESS (2026-06-10, drop 2): UI-7 WEB ONBOARDING DESIGNED + SHIPPED this drop per Brian's retention mandate ('handled well, and thoroughly'). Design contract: web_onboarding_design_v1.md (repo root). Three layers: (A) training side panel driven by the new onboarding_state push (chain name, step rail n/M, title, NPC briefing, objective, teaches chips staging corpus-authored tokens + ' ', ATTEMPT chip for skill_check steps staging the real `chain attempt`, step-complete flash, one-time graduation card); (B) finite coach pulses on EXISTING quick-action anchors only (look->LOOK, say/talk->SAY, +bounties->JOBS); (C) first-run 4-mark client tour, once per browser via localStorage (cosmetic state), only while a chain is active, replayable via the panel-head '?'. Producer = chain_events.build_onboarding_state layered on get_active_step_info (extended ADDITIVELY — chain status/attempt unaffected); push = session._hud_sidebar_onboarding with the _last_chain_step graduation-once memo (reconnect after graduation = silence). KEY AUDIT FINDING: the tutorial SYSTEM was already fully live (9 chains, chargen-start, 11 hooks, graduation, chain status parity) — get_active_step_info's docstring claimed 'Used by web HUD' but nothing did; UI-7 fills that seam. Phase 2 candidates logged (in_step_location hint, tutorial_v2 elective rail, missions modal) — flagged, not half-built. Remaining: UI-8 crafting (after T2.CRAFT.integration_design_pass); commissary fold-in gated on UI-4b.commissary_vendor_merge (pending Brian). --- prior same-day drop 1: UI-5 bounty board + UI-6 objective/juice shipped; _hud_active_jobs bounty-branch fix; protocol ledger v1_4 reconstructed after the 06-07 phantom. --- prior 2026-06-07: UI-1..UI-4b + equipment untangle shipped. Build order UI-1 -> UI-7 done; then UI-8.
tier_2_queued.blocking: SEQUENCED AFTER Sourcebook Enrichment Lanes A-F are complete (Brian: 'as soon as we finish this enrichment implementation (A-F), I'd like to pivot back to the UI'). Connected to T2.DIFF.difficulty_tiers_design (zone difficulty indicators are one of the UI surfaces). Web UI is plausibly make-or-break for the target audience (Brian: 'Different audience, not the same MUSHers I grew up with' — matches Claude's original web-first call).
tier_2_queued.goal: Produce a DETAILED package that Claude Design can consume to expand the web SPA UI for gameplay surfaces that are currently text-command-only. Claude (this project) knows the gameplay; Claude Design does NOT — so the package must teach it each surface: what the system does, the current text commands/flows, what server data is already exposed (and what needs a protocol addition), the proposed web interaction model, and explicitly what stays text.
tier_2_queued.hard_principle: Posing / speaking / emotes / free-text RP STAY TEXT — that free-text channel is the soul of a MUSH and the web UI must augment it, never replace it. Web UI is for MECHANICAL STATE and STRUCTURED interactions (browse/select/confirm/manage), shown alongside the RP text stream.
tier_2_queued.reference_point: The just-shipped vendor-interaction UX is the pattern to extend. Inventory is the obvious adjacent surface; Brian's bet (shared by Claude) is there is a LOT more.
candidate_surfaces_claude_enumerated: Inventory + equip/unequip + containers + get/drop/give (Brian's named adjacent surface).
Character sheet — already has SPA tier renderers + a sheet_redesign_design; EXTEND, don't restart (attributes/skills/Force skills/wounds/CP/FP/DSP/equipment).

Combat HUD — initiative order, range bands, wound/stun track, the NEW special-attack conditions (poison stacks, grapple/constriction restraint), action declaration (attack/dodge/parry/full-dodge/flee) as buttons. Posing/called shots stay text; the mechanical state is ideal for UI. High-impact, adjacent to just-shipped combat work.
Space + SPACE COMBAT — ship position/range/shields/hull/weapon arcs/sensors/hyperspace/cargo. Space combat especially benefits from a spatial UI.
Crafting + mining/harvest — recipe browser, material counts, build queue + progress (gundark_crafting_integration, sourcebook_mining_crafting_exp).
Economy — banking/ledger, the COMING +market (Drop 5), the bounty-hunter board, org treasury, P2P transfers. Markets/boards/banking are classic web surfaces.
Force-power palette — list + activate, control/sense/alter grouping.
CP spend / training + the milestone meter (progression_gates).
Org/faction — roster, ranks, reputation, treasury dashboards (faction_reputation, organizations_factions).
Territory control — map overlay of zone control state (map/POI work already exists; extend it).
Player city / housing / shop OWNER dashboards — plots, buildings, construction, stock, pricing.
Padawan/Master pairing + Jedi-Village & Spacer quest journals.
Tutorial / onboarding — a web onboarding flow is one of the biggest wins for the new (non-MUSHer) audience.

Medical/healing/respawn status; channels/mail/news/BBS (classic web surfaces); sabacc-den minigame + entertainer performance; security-zone + DIFFICULTY indicators (ties to T2.DIFF); minimap/navigation.
tier_2_queued.room_presentation_note: Keep room DESCRIPTIONS as text, BUT consider a persistent 'current room' panel (name, exits, occupants, objects, POIs) so room state is always visible. This also sidesteps a Brian-flagged client behavior: on movement the text pane does NOT reliably auto-scroll to show the new room's surroundings. INVESTIGATE + FIX the scroll-on-move behavior, and evaluate whether a structured room header/panel is the better long-term answer than relying on scroll. (Possible standalone bug — could be triaged earlier if it bites in testing.)
tier_2_queued.deliverable_form: A doc (or doc set) Claude Design can work from: per-surface spec (system summary, current text flow, server data available / protocol gap vs web_client_vision_and_protocol_v1_3, proposed web model, what-stays-text), PRIORITIZED (P1 high-impact + adjacent-to-shipped: inventory, combat HUD, market/bounty board, crafting; P2; P3). Must reference the existing SPA architecture (m3_* modules, m3_tier_registry getTierRenderer, the vision/protocol doc) so Claude Design EXTENDS the established client, not reinvents it.
tier_2_queued.claude_todo_flag: Per Brian's standing 'do not blindly implement TODO as written' rule: re-examine at build time. Workflow = (1) Claude produces the package, (2) Brian prioritizes, (3) implementation drops follow. Surface the surface-list to Brian for additions/cuts before writing the full package.

tier_2_queued.title: Difficulty tiers / zoned content progression (DESIGN) — newbie vs mid vs end-game areas (Brian floated, 2026-06-06)
tier_2_queued.effort: medium-large (design + a short doc, then world-data + tiered spawn-tables + map/zone UI surfacing)
tier_2_queued.status: queued — DIRECTION APPROVED 2026-06-07 (D2: extend the existing security-zone + wilderness-region machinery; no parallel system). STILL NEEDS the six sub-decisions (taxonomy/count, difficulty-vs-security, gate-vs-advisory, per-world tier map, space-lane tiering, newbie PvP-safety) + a short difficulty_tiers_design doc before build.
tier_2_queued.blocking: Pre-launch. Independent of but CONNECTED to T2.UIPKG (zone difficulty indicators are a UI surface). Brian invited counter-proposals ('if you have something better I'm all ears').
tier_2_queued.problem_brian: No difficulty levels today. New players should be able to experience combat (and space combat) in a NEWBIE area, mid-game players in another, end-game in another. Wilderness should have SECTIONS with KNOWN lines (players see where the tiers are). PC cities should probably only sit in mid-to-high difficulty areas.
tier_2_queued.claude_recommendation: EXTEND, don't add (consistent with the 'Lane D plugs into existing faction-intent machinery' discipline): ride the EXISTING security-zone model + wilderness region/biome machinery rather than build a parallel difficulty system. Attach a threat/difficulty BAND to zones (e.g. Newcomer / Seasoned / Veteran, or a numeric threat rating), SURFACED on the map + room/zone header (the UI tie-in). Gate creature-spawn danger and space-patrol threat by zone tier — the Lane-A creatures already carry a danger profile, so build TIERED spawn tables over them. Restrict PC-city placement to mid+ tiers so newbie areas stay controlled/protected. Wire into the security/territory-control overlay already built.
open_decisions_for_brian: Tier taxonomy + count (named bands vs numeric threat rating).
Is difficulty == security level, or a separate parallel axis layered on zones?
How visibly to mark the lines — hard gates (can't enter) vs advisory (warned but allowed)?
Which of the 6 launch worlds host which tiers (Tatooine/Nar Shaddaa/Coruscant/Kuat/Kamino/Geonosis)?
Space-lane tiering — do space routes get the same bands?
Are newbie tiers PvP-safe (intersect with the +pvp opt-in already shipped)?
tier_2_queued.next_step: Once Brian picks a direction, write a short difficulty_tiers_design doc, then sequence the world-data + spawn-table + UI work (after Lanes A-F, alongside / feeding the UI package).
