_notes: Numeric values picked during feature shipping where Brian has NOT explicitly signed off. Each entry names the value, where it lives in code/data, what it currently is, what alternative values were considered, and the smallest possible test or playtest that would confirm or refute the pick.
Updated at end of every drop alongside CHANGELOG.md. New entries appended as SYN.7+ drops introduce new tuning knobs.

The whole-game economist pass (T2.ECON.review) RAN on 2026-05-31 (SW_MUSH_Economy_Audit_FINAL.md) and is closed. These remaining numeric picks are now post-launch tuning knobs: they feed T3.19 (tunables externalization + telemetry-grounded tuning), where real player data — not a static review — calibrates them. Kept here as the bookkeeping ledger so Brian sees the picks before they compound.

alternatives: 24h per-character GLOBAL (any landmark consumes the daily) — tighter; 1 kyber/Jedi/day across the map
Weekly per-character — ceremonial; 1 kyber/Jedi/week aligns with Padawan-trial narrative
24h per-landmark per-character (current) — 4 force-resonant landmarks in CW × 24h = 4 shards/Jedi/day max

No cooldown but per-landmark consumed-this-week tag (region quality model) — landmark goes quiet for the week after first attune by any character; preserves single-Jedi pacing but adds cross-Jedi competition
syn6_questions.supply_math: Current: 4 shards/Jedi/day theoretical max (need to visit 4 landmarks). Lightsaber needs 1 kyber. So one Jedi can craft 4 lightsabers/day or accumulate stacks indefinitely. Likely oversupplied for the one-and-done canonical need.
syn6_questions.concern: HIGH PRIORITY — most likely to be off. Per-landmark cooldown was the easy implementation choice; per-character (or per-week) is probably the right design choice for scarcity. Need a Brian call on whether kyber is 'common-for-Jedi' or 'ceremonial-for-Jedi'.

syn6_questions.value: q75 floor, q95 ceiling, +5Q per skill margin band
alternatives: Floor at q60, ceiling at q90 — broader band, some duds; matches existing crafting tier descriptors (Good=60+, Superior=80+, Masterwork=90+)
Floor at q75 (current, matches T5_MIN_QUALITY), ceiling q95 — every successful attune is T5-craftable; no duds
Floor at q70, ceiling q100, margin scaling — lets exceptional rolls cap at q100 but removes the SYN.8 reserved-tier semantics
Variable per-landmark (some landmarks 'minor', some 'major' kyber) — content-authoring layer
syn6_questions.supply_math: Current: every successful attune yields T5-craftable material. A skilled Jedi (8D Scholar) auto-passes DC 11 every time. No duds means every cooldown turns into supply.
syn6_questions.concern: No-duds design is generous. Pre-launch playtest should confirm: is the right shape 'always get something' (current) or 'sometimes get nothing'?

syn6_questions.value: DC 11 (Moderate), Knowledge attribute or scholar/willpower skill
alternatives: DC 11 (Moderate, current) — typical Jedi clears easily
DC 15 (Difficult) — Padawan strains, Knight passes, Master auto-passes; tighter gate on quality outcomes
DC 21 (Very Difficult) — only Masters reliably succeed; cap matches T5 schematic-difficulty band; very ceremonial
syn6_questions.concern: Current DC 11 means a typical Jedi-faction starter character (3D Knowledge) succeeds ~50%, a trained one (4D+) succeeds nearly always. Pairs with the no-duds quality floor to give consistent yield.

alternatives: 30 min per-region (current) — design doc value
30 min global — prevents region-cycling farm; tighter session loop but penalizes legitimate roaming play
60 min per-region + 30 min global floor — caps both cycling and single-spot farming
Reset on death — combat-tied cooldown
syn6_questions.supply_math: Current: a player cycling through 5+ wilderness regions per session could harvest 5+ × yield/30min — accumulates credits + resources at session-length pace. No global supply ceiling.
syn6_questions.concern: 30min/region was the design number; per-region was the right axis. But the lack of a session-level ceiling means a determined harvester can fill inventory in one play session. Maybe acceptable; needs playtest.

alternatives: 10% — softer visitor friction
15% (current, design number)
20-25% — heavier owner advantage, more incentive to claim
Variable by region security (lawless 10%, contested 20%) — content-axis dial
syn6_questions.concern: 15% is the design number, no debate needed unless the economist pass surfaces it as a friction issue.

alternatives: 0.7×..1.3× (current, design number) — ±30% swing
0.8×..1.2× — tamer, less crafter-travel motivation
0.5×..1.5× — wider, stronger 'follow the news' pull
syn6_questions.concern: Design number; preserve unless economist pass calls for tightening.

syn6_questions.value: 6-row table per design §2.5.2 (Contested foothold 100-200cr → Lawless control 400-800cr)
alternatives: Design numbers (current)
Halved for soft-launch tuning
Doubled for 'cash-rich crafter' lane
syn6_questions.concern: Pairs with weekly variance + skill-margin scaling. End-to-end credit-per-hour-of-harvest math hasn't been computed. Economist pass should derive: typical Jedi-faction Knight playing 1h, harvesting 2 regions, gets X credits. Then compare against vendor prices, weapon costs, ship maintenance, etc.

syn6_questions.value: +20% credits per 5-pt margin band, no cap
alternatives: +20% per band uncapped (current) — a 50-margin roll gives 11× the base credits
+20% per band capped at 5 bands (+100%) — exceptional rolls double the base, no more
+10% per band, capped at +50% — softer reward curve
syn6_questions.concern: Current uncapped scaling means a Master-tier harvester can get extreme credit outliers. Tests use 12D Survival which produces consistent +large margins. Real play might be tamer, but a 10D+ Survival skill is achievable post-progression.

alternatives: 10% (current) — rare but reachable; +1 rare stack at q100 per ~10 control-tier harvests
5% — half as common; aligns with the 'reserved for SYN.8 Tier-3 anomaly drops' framing of q100
20% — twice as common; tempts more Control-tier-region traffic
syn6_questions.concern: Today this is the only path to q100 'rare' stacks (the post-launch T5 recipes won't use 'rare' as a primary mat anyway). Will become moot when SYN.8 lands and q100 'rare' is design-reserved for anomaly drops — at that point reduce or remove.

alternatives: 5% — half as common; closer to peer T5 scarcity
7% (current) — ~1 per ~7h solo harvesting
10% — matches generic t5_rare; risks Coruscant becoming the path of least resistance for the whole T5 lane
syn6_questions.concern: Only faucet for scavenged_republic_tech; sole sink is t5_hyperdrive_surge_converter (12,000cr). Solo-harvestable, deliberately easier than the participation-gated peer T5 mats per the non-PvP-crafter design (§2.5.6).

alternatives: 25-28 (current) — Master crafter with 8D+ skill + Wild Die explosion typical
30-32 (Heroic) — even Masters need exceptional rolls; pairs better with 'genuine endgame' framing
20-23 (Difficult top) — overlaps existing T4 ceiling; might dilute the T5 boundary
syn6_questions.concern: T1-T4 ceiling is 20. T5 at 25-28 is clearly above. Pre-launch playtest with a Master crafter should confirm success rates feel right (target: 50-70% success at intended progression tier).

syn6_questions.value: T5 mat q75+, standard T1-T4 components at q50-65
alternatives: Current: q75 T5 mat + q50-65 standard mats
Tighter: q75 T5 mat + q65-75 standard mats — pulls in more standard-material economy
Looser: q75 T5 mat + q40-50 standard mats — only the T5 mat matters; standard mats are filler
syn6_questions.concern: Current bands mean a Master crafter who can't source q50+ standard materials is locked out even with T5 mats. Pairs with weekly variance (which can push a region's metal to 0.7× → harder to find q50+ stacks). Real bottleneck question is whether the standard-mat floor is the right shape.

alternatives: 1 per craft (current) — minimum scarcity
2-3 per craft for more potent items (e.g. master-grade armor) — differentiates T5 outputs by gating cost
Variable by output_type — lightsaber 1, ship part 2, armor 1
syn6_questions.concern: Flat 1-per-craft means every T5 output costs the same in T5-mat terms. Differentiation could come from T1-T4 component costs (current shape) or from T5-mat costs.

syn6_questions.value: Region-scoped fallback — no flags in region → every room qualifies
alternatives: Region-scoped fallback (current) — incremental authoring; un-audited regions stay open
Global fallback — only triggers if NO room anywhere has the flag
Hard gate, no fallback — all rooms require the flag from day one
syn6_questions.concern: Current allows content team to migrate one region at a time without breaking unmarked regions. Fallback path goes dormant once all regions are flagged (post-launch content pass). Acceptable as a transitional design; economist pass should confirm long-term shape after content pass completes.

tunable_open_questions.syn7_questions_pending: Will be appended as SYN.7.b drops land — Tier 2 anomaly reward shape (15-25 influence is the design number, but the per-tier loot tables, multi-participant distribution rules, and corvette-boarding-ground-adaptation specifics all need explicit picks).

syn7a_questions.value: hourly tick + 0.4 spawn-chance → expected ~2.5h average interval
alternatives: hourly tick + 0.4 chance (current) → matches design '2-3h per region'
hourly tick + 0.5 chance → 2h avg (faster, more visible activity for small playerbase)
hourly tick + 0.33 chance → 3h avg (slower, more scarce)
30-min tick + variable chance — finer control but tighter polling load
syn7a_questions.supply_math: With 2 wilderness regions (Tatooine Dune Sea, Coruscant Underworld) and 0.4 spawn-chance per region per hour, expected ~0.8 anomaly spawns/hour across the world. Per-region cap MAX_PER_REGION=2 means a single region tops out at 2 concurrent. For a small playerbase that's about right; for a quiet server with 1-2 active players a 2.5h cadence means a player playing 1 hour rarely sees a fresh spawn during their session.
syn7a_questions.concern: Design number aligned; needs playtest to confirm 'feels right' density. Spawn-chance 0.4 was picked to hit the design's 2-3h midpoint; legitimate tunable. Halving the chance (0.2 → 5h avg) might better match a sparse server.

alternatives: 30 min (current, design number)
60 min — gives players a wider response window
15 min — tighter urgency, harder to catch
syn7a_questions.concern: Design number. Preserve unless playtest shows players consistently arrive too late.

alternatives: 2 (current) — prevents accumulation during inactive periods
1 — strict pacing, one-at-a-time
3-5 — allows backlog for quiet periods
syn7a_questions.concern: 2 is a reasonable cap. With ~30 min duration and ~2.5h cadence, a region naturally hovers near 0-1 anomalies most of the time. Cap mostly relevant for the early-game cold-start window when no one's resolving.

alternatives: +5 (current, design number)
+3-7 variable by template difficulty — finer-grained pacing
+2-3 — even tighter; combined with high cadence this nudges toward 'lots of small wins' play
syn7a_questions.concern: Design number. The supply math depends on resolution rate: a faction with one active player resolving every anomaly in their territory gets +5 inf × N anomalies × resolve rate. At ~2.5h/region cadence and ~2 regions in active use, that's +5 × 8/day × 0.5 resolve rate × 2 regions = ~40 inf/day per region per active faction. Foothold (50) reached in ~6 days, Dominant (200) in ~5 weeks. Pre-launch playtest should confirm this curve is right for the intended progression.

alternatives: DC 13 (current) — Moderate-Difficult; starter 3D character rolls average 10-11 → fails most; trained 4D-5D rolls 14-17 → succeeds most
DC 11 — Moderate; even starter chars succeed roughly half the time
DC 15 — Difficult; tightly gated, only trained characters reliably succeed
syn7a_questions.concern: DC 13 is meant to be 'newer players sometimes fail, trained players reliably succeed.' Pairs with one-shot semantics (failed resolution still consumes the anomaly + grants reduced reward). Playtest target: ~70% success rate for the intended-tier player (4D in the relevant skill).

syn7a_questions.value: Per-template credit bands (100-500cr range) + 1-4 resource stacks at q30-60
alternatives: Current bands (100-500cr success, 0-100cr fail; 1-4 stacks at q30-60)
Wider success-vs-fail gap — make success much more rewarding, fail nearly nothing
Higher quality on resources — q50-70 instead of q30-60 — feeds T5 crafting more aggressively
Lower credit, higher resource — emphasizes the crafter-pipeline aspect
syn7a_questions.concern: Picked to feel proportionate to the time/risk: ~30 min effort, skill check vs DC 13, +5 influence. Compare against harvest yields (100-800cr per harvest with 30-min regional cooldown) — anomalies should sit in the same band as harvest but with the influence kicker as the differentiator. End-to-end credit-per-hour math hasn't been computed; defer to economist pass.

syn7a_questions.value: Failed skill check still grants partial reward (anomaly consumed)
alternatives: Current: one-shot, fail consumes anomaly + partial reward (anti-farm + low frustration)
Fail leaves anomaly active for others — encourages handoff but lets first-arriver retry
Fail consumes anomaly + no reward — punishing; might feel bad
No skill check, just walk-up resolution — drops the resolution mechanic entirely
syn7a_questions.concern: One-shot semantics prevents farming and prevents player A failing then player B succeeding (which would feel arbitrary). Partial reward on fail keeps players engaged — failing still feels worthwhile. Tusken party fail_reward is 0/0/0 because narratively losing a combat shouldn't pay out; that's an intentional asymmetry within the catalog.

alternatives: Uniform (current) — every template equally likely
Region-flavored weighting — Tusken party more likely in Dune Sea, CIS probe more likely in CIS-traffic regions
Faction-state weighting — Director sees active CW conflict in region → CIS probe more likely
Rarity-weighted — common templates 30%, rare 10%
syn7a_questions.concern: Uniform is the simple shape. Region-flavored weighting would be a SYN.7.b polish drop and ties into the Director AI's existing event-weighting mechanics. Defer until Tier 2 templates land so region-specific weighting can be designed once across both tiers.

tunable_open_questions.syn8_questions_pending: Will be appended as SYN.8 drops — Tier 3 world boss numbers (HP, multi-phase HP thresholds, participation scaling, kyber-pearl drop count formula floor(participants/4) — design number, but the participant denominator could be tighter or looser).
tunable_open_questions.syn9_questions_pending: Will be appended as SYN.9 drops — building construction costs, category effect magnitudes, construction time, demolish refund.

tier_3_post_launch.title: Padawan-Master post-launch expansion (council, lineage trees, re-assignment)
Cities post-launch expansion (multi-city-per-org, paged list sub-modals, P2P discovery)
Pre-launch tunables externalization + telemetry instrumentation (knob config files + post-launch tuning loop)
tier_3_post_launch.timing: NEAR-LAUNCH refactor (the externalization), then POST-LAUNCH ongoing (the tuning loop). Brian's framing 2026-06-06: 'near the end of development, before we go live.'
tier_3_post_launch.goal: Two halves. (1) EXTERNALIZE every tuning lever (the magic numbers currently living as module-level constants / inline literals across the engine) into versioned configuration files, so a knob can be tweaked without a code edit or a redeploy-to-change-a-number. (2) Add ROBUST, STRUCTURED logging/telemetry at the decision points those levers govern, so after launch Claude can read the telemetry and recommend knob changes grounded in real player behavior.
tier_3_post_launch.knob_domains_to_sweep: Non-exhaustive starting catalog (grep these out of the modules and lift to config): ECONOMY — adjust_credits faucet/sink rates, P2P_DAILY_CAP, trade SUPPLY_POOL/DEMAND_POOL sizes + refresh timers + volume_premium curve, FINE_FRACTION / smuggling EV, vendor salvage-price scaling + listing TTL, sink prices (home-prestige/vanity-title/commissary tiers, spacedock fee), gear-insurance premium, city tax rates. DIFFICULTY — security-zone combat gates, customs Perception/Con difficulties + infraction fines, wilderness encounter weights + creature spawn counts, hazard-table thresholds, NPC archetype hostility, space astrogation/scan difficulties. PROGRESSION — CP award rates + milestone thresholds (ships_log + the milestone-CP meter), chain rewards, faction-rep tier breakpoints + shop modifiers, Padawan/Master bond pacing. FORCE — DSP award/decay rates, mind-influence opposed-roll baselines, the DSP-bounty trigger threshold. DIRECTOR — alert-level thresholds, world-event fire probabilities + durations + dormant-effect multipliers (bounty_reward_mult, pirate/patrol_spawn_mult, etc.), milestone faction-average thresholds. SPAWN/POPULATION — NPC counts, traffic-ship density, idle-queue cadence. PERSISTENT-THREAT KNOBS (flagged by Brian 2026-06-06 as a concrete example — 'hunter aggression is one of the variables I'd want a config to tune: how strong are they? how often will they pursue a given PC?'): the roaming DSP bounty hunter's AGGRESSION — per-DSP-tier combat strength (engine/dsp_hunter.py hunter_combat_sheet, blaster 5D/6D/7D), pursuit CADENCE (step_for_dsp 5/9/14, advance_progress clamp 0..100, the dsp_hunter_tick interval=120/offset=75, the at_heels live-spawn gate), and the wanted threshold (bounty_board.DSP_BOUNTY_THRESHOLD=4); plus the cult COMMUNAL OBJECTIVE knobs (engine/communal_objective.py MENACE_START 35 / MENACE_PER_MINUTE 0.35 / DEADLINE_HOURS 48 / STRIKE_COOLDOWN_S 600 / strike difficulty 10/13/16 / reduction bands 5/8/11/14 / REP_FLOOR 3..REP_MAX 15 / TITLE_SHARE_THRESHOLD 0.10 / the runtime REPOST_COOLDOWN 6h) and the smuggling checkpoint scrutiny (engine/smuggling.py CHECKPOINT_PATROL_BOOST 0.15 + CHECKPOINT_DIFFICULTY_BOOST 3, already named constants, alongside the still-inline lockdown 0.30/+5).
tier_3_post_launch.approach_notes: Phase it, no big-bang. (a) Inventory pass: grep the tuning literals per domain; pick a format — YAML fits the existing content pipeline (one file per domain: economy_tuning.yaml / difficulty_tuning.yaml / progression_tuning.yaml / force_tuning.yaml / director_tuning.yaml, or a tuning/ dir). (b) Loader with SAFE DEFAULTS: every knob keeps its current in-code value as the default so a missing/partial config can never brick the game (fail-safe, like the era/lore loaders). (c) Hot-reload is nice-to-have, not required for v1 (restart-to-apply is fine for tuning). (d) Telemetry: structured (JSON-line) events at each governed decision — emit inputs + the rolled/derived output + the knob values in effect, so a recommendation can say 'at knob X=N the outcome distribution was Y.' Reuse the existing logging infra; add a dedicated telemetry sink. (e) Guard tests: config round-trips; a missing config falls back to in-code defaults; telemetry emitters fire with the expected schema.
tier_3_post_launch.ties_into: Absorbs the ad-hoc TUNABLE notes scattered through _notes (gear-insurance premium, prestige steepness, vanity costs, commissary, FINE_FRACTION, P2P cap, vendor TTL) and the tunable_open_questions block — those become entries in the new config files. Natural home for the Director AI tuning knobs (T3.15).
tier_3_post_launch.blocks_or_after: AFTER feature-complete — this is a hardening/operability pass, not new gameplay. Do NOT start mid-feature: it should sweep a SETTLED codebase so the knob inventory is stable. The telemetry half keeps paying off for the life of the live game.
tier_3_post_launch.telemetry_purpose_clarified: Brian 2026-06-06: the telemetry half exists to collect data USEFUL TO CLAUDE for post-launch analysis -> recommend config-knob changes (the T3.19 levers) to keep the game on track / more FUN (Brian's explicit, admittedly-vague goal). Capture broadly now; Claude analyzes dumps offline post-launch. Implementation posture: append-only JSON-line events to a dedicated sink, high-frequency events SAMPLED, all emitters async/buffered/non-blocking (T3.21 guards this) -- NEVER slow the game loop. Aggregation is offline, not in-process.

telemetry_metrics_catalog.A_engagement_and_fun_proxies: Session-length distribution; sessions/player/week; D1/D7/D30 return rate; time-of-day concurrency (a MUSH needs critical-mass population to be fun); time-from-login-to-first-meaningful-action (onboarding friction); and the LAST ACTION before a player stops returning (a 'fun-cliff' detector -- what were they doing when the game lost them).
telemetry_metrics_catalog.B_pacing_progression_economy: CP earn-rate vs spend-rate + time-to-milestone distributions (detect grind or stall); credit-balance distribution + wealth Gini over time (inflation/inequality, the economist's worry now measurable); PER-FAUCET inflow + PER-SINK outflow rates (which faucets dominate, which sinks are vestigial -- directly maps to a config knob); skill/attribute advancement curves + how long players sit at each power tier.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.C_content_alive_utilization: Command-frequency histogram (a dead feature shows up as ~0 use); per-SYSTEM engagement split (combat / crafting / smuggling / sabacc / Force / social / exploration -- is the cross-playstyle design actually working or does one loop dominate?); room/zone/world visit heatmap (find the ghost towns); mission/quest started->completed->abandoned funnels (abandonment = friction or boredom); cult communal-objective participation rate + win/lose ratio + contribution spread; DSP-hunter spawn -> beaten vs collected.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.D_friction_and_frustration: Combat win/loss/flee rates by encounter type; PC-death frequency + CAUSE (creature/PvP/hazard/hunter); skill-check success rates by difficulty band (are DCs calibrated -- too-easy is boring, too-hard frustrates); a 'STUCK' signal = the same failed action repeated N times (a wall players can't pass -- quest/tutorial friction); command-error / 'huh?' rate per command (UX friction, which verbs confuse); a rage-quit proxy = logoff immediately after a death/loss/failure.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.E_social_fabric_MUSH: For a MUSH, social IS the fun. say/pose/emote/channel volume; multi-player interaction density + social-graph breadth (distinct partners per player); solo vs grouped activity ratio; NEW-PLAYER social integration (does a newcomer get talked to / grouped within session 1 -- one of the biggest MUSH retention drivers); entertainer/audience-system usage; RP-scene length/frequency.
telemetry_metrics_catalog.F_operational_perf_and_noslowdown_guard: Per-command latency p50/p95/p99; tick duration; DB-query times; SPA wire round-trip; error/exception rate per subsystem. Doubles as the perf-regression detector AND the guardrail that the telemetry itself + the game stay fast (the no-slowdown caveat, enforced under T3.21).
telemetry_metrics_catalog.STANDOUT_outside_the_box_fun_proxies: (1) VALENCE DISAMBIGUATION via the follow-on action -- a death/failure followed by IMMEDIATE RETRY reads as challenge-fun ('I'll get it this time'); the SAME death followed by LOGOFF reads as frustration. Identical event, opposite valence; the next action tells you which. Probably the single most useful fun-vs-frustration signal. (2) VOLUNTARY REPETITION -- a player re-running a loop (smuggling run, hunt, sabacc hand) UNPROMPTED with no quest forcing it = that loop is fun. (3) 'ONE MORE TURN' -- activity in the final minutes before logoff / continuing past a natural stopping point = the compulsion loop is working. (4) LOGIN-TIMING AROUND EVENTS -- do players log in MORE around scheduled cult uprisings / world events? Anticipation = the events are a genuine draw. (5) INTRINSIC GOALS -- time on activities with NO mechanical reward (pure RP, exploration, base decoration, helping newbies). Intrinsic motivation is the deepest fun signal there is. (6) CHAT-AFFECT SPIKES -- clustering of exclamations / caps / laughter-emotes / rapid poses around an event = a crude 'something exciting just happened' detector; correlate the spike with its trigger to locate the fun MOMENTS. (7) VARIETY-SEEKING vs ROTE-GRIND -- a player trying NEW systems/commands over time = curiosity/engagement; a narrow loop forever is ambiguous (fun-grind or boredom) -- disambiguate with the session-length TREND (lengthening = fun, shortening = burning out). (8) SOCIAL CONTAGION -- does one player's act (starting a brawl, posting a bounty, rallying the cult) pull OTHERS into the same activity within a window? High contagion = the feature creates emergent, shared fun.

tier_3_post_launch.BEFORE_IMPLEMENTING_REVIEW: Brian 2026-06-06: the telemetry_metrics_catalog above is a FIRST-DRAFT brainstorm, NOT a build spec. Do NOT blindly implement it as written. Claude must RE-REVIEW and EXPAND the metric list at implementation time, with ECONOMY depth as the explicit gap (Brian's focus area; the current bucket B is thin for an economy-heavy game). Economy directions to flesh out then: per-good price stability + supply/demand pool levels over time, trade volume by route, realized smuggling EV vs theoretical, per-SINK engagement (which sinks actually drain credits vs are vestigial), per-FAUCET share, sabacc/gambling flows, NPC-buyback usage, P2P transfer patterns (also RMT/exploit detection), wealth Gini trend. More broadly: this 're-think before building, don't blindly implement the TODO' discipline applies to ALL design-heavy TODO items, not just telemetry.

tier_3_post_launch.title: State-preservation / safe-migration robustness pass
tier_3_post_launch.timing: PRE-LAUNCH hardening, clustered with T3.19 (Brian 2026-06-06: 'right before or after we deal with telemetry and config'). After features are stable.
tier_3_post_launch.goal: Make the codebase robust so that ANY post-launch schema change or invasive change has a CLEAR, TESTED path that PRESERVES all players' live game state -- no data loss, no silently-dropped fields on upgrade. This is the safety net for the unavoidable post-launch changes; it's the direct reason Brian wants nearly everything implemented pre-launch (to minimize how often this net is needed).
tier_3_post_launch.scope_notes: (a) Migration-framework audit: migrations forward-only, idempotent, versioned, and each one REVERSIBLE-or-documented. (b) A migration test harness that runs every migration against a POPULATED/seeded DB (real characters, orgs, ships, inventories) -- not just a fresh DB -- and asserts no row/field loss; this is what catches the dangerous ones. (c) Defensive (de)serialization discipline: from_db_dict already tolerates partial/pre-migration rows; codify that as a contract and extend it everywhere. F.7.n-class bugs (DERIVED state that doesn't survive reload) live here -- a reload-round-trip invariant test per persisted entity. (d) Backup/restore + a documented upgrade runbook. (e) A DB-integrity validator that can scan a live DB for corruption/orphans before & after a migration. (f) A written 'state-preservation contract' that every future schema PR must honor (candidate arch invariant at the v52 consolidation).
tier_3_post_launch.ties_into: F.7.n (a Claude-FLAGGED, UNCONFIRMED force_sensitive-on-reload concern that Brian has NEVER observed -- VERIFY it here via a reload-round-trip test; CLOSE as not-a-bug if FS survives, rather than 'fixing' a bug that may not exist); the arch-doc v52 reconciliation (add the state-preservation invariant); db/database.py SCHEMA_VERSION + MIGRATIONS.

tier_3_post_launch.title: Full optimization + security pass
tier_3_post_launch.timing: PRE-LAUNCH hardening, after features are stable (optimize a SETTLED codebase) and after T3.19 (so it can also verify telemetry adds no latency). Brian 2026-06-06.
tier_3_post_launch.goal: Two halves. OPTIMIZATION: profile and tighten hot paths so the live game stays fast under load (and so the T3.19 telemetry is provably non-blocking -- the no-slowdown caveat). SECURITY: harden the full attack surface before exposing the game to the public internet.
tier_3_post_launch.scope_notes: OPTIMIZATION -- profile tick handlers, the combat resolver, the SPA wire protocol, and DB access (indexes, N+1 queries, aiosqlite patterns, connection reuse); make every telemetry emitter async/buffered/sampled; watch hot-path allocation + memory growth over a long session. SECURITY -- input validation across ALL command parsers + the web API; SQL-parameterization audit (mostly done via the ledger work -- verify globally); auth/session/token handling; rate-limiting beyond P2P_DAILY_CAP (per-command + per-API); the aiohttp surface (CORS, headers, request-size/DoS limits); secrets handling; an AccessLevel-enforcement audit on every admin/Director command; the web-client XSS/injection surface (the SPA renders server strings).
tier_3_post_launch.ties_into: T3.19 telemetry (must be non-blocking); the web_client protocol; AccessLevel discipline; the existing P2P rate-limit as the rate-limiting precedent.

design_calls_pending_brian.question: scavenged_republic_tech harvests on the generic Survival check (DC 6). Flavor-wise, salvaging Republic tech reads as Technical (salvage) or Search, not wilderness Survival. Ship Survival now (engine hardwires HARVEST_SKILL), or invest post-launch in a per-region harvest-skill override?
design_calls_pending_brian.recommendation: Survival now (consistent with the harvest engine; Easy-6 is WEG-ladder-correct); per-region skill override is a larger change — defer to post-launch flavor polish. Low priority; not launch-blocking.

design_calls_pending_brian.question: Should commissary/faction-issued gear be sellable back, at what price, and is it bind-on-pickup? npc_refuses_buyback only gates player-crafted quality>=50 items, so it does NOT cover faction-issued gear. Questions: (1) Can a player sell back faction gear to any vendor? (2) At what rate (e.g. 50% of commissary cost)? (3) Is faction-issued gear bind-on-pickup (cannot trade/sell at all)? These three sub-decisions gate any sellback drop.

design_calls_resolved_recent.decision: a + vendor flag — open-market buying requires an in-room NPC with ai_config.vendor: true (NOT merely Bargain-skilled: vendors disentangled from hagglers; Lup haggles without becoming an arms dealer unless flagged). Haggle uses the vendor's own Bargain, generic 3D only when the vendor lacks the skill. Curated 8 vendors so every major settlement has a buy point; Kamino deliberately none (commissary world); Gundark teaches, never retails (pinned). Option (b) shop-room flag remains available later as an additive kiosk exception.

Thermal detonator STAYS UNCRAFTABLE even at the black-market tier. Rationale: the X-class icon's scarcity is its identity — vendor/loot-only preserves mystique, and a craftable 10D would dominate the ordnance economy. Drop D's uncraftable pin stands; flip it in a future drop if Brian vetoes.

a — trainer-taught schematics cost TUITION: 50% of the schematic base_cost, minimum 50 cr, charged via adjust_credits(char, -tuition, "schematic_tuition") (a real credit SINK). PC-to-PC teaching stays FREE. Logged caveat honored: tutorial chains ("talk trainer -> craft") must not wall a broke newbie — mechanism decided at implementation (first-lesson-free per trainer recommended). Implementation lands in Gundark Drop G.

a — REMOVE the hard P2P daily cap; KEEP the 5% transaction tax (p2p_tax sink) and the p2p_transfer ledger tag; CONVERT the old threshold into an @economy velocity ALERT (admin-visible, never commerce-blocking). Rationale: segmentation (a) makes PCs the supply chain; a single quality-crafted item legitimately trades above 1,500 cr. Alt-funneling is fought at the faucet throttle + alerts, not by capping legitimate trade. Reverses audit v2 §2.4 with explicit sign-off.

a — adopt availability-tier vendor segmentation: Avail-1 vendor-stocked at book cost AND craftable; Avail-2/3 NOT routinely vendor-stocked (craft/loot/player-shop supply); Avail-4/X = Drop G black-market loop. Implementation gates Lane C vendor placement + Drop G; starts with an existing-shop stock audit and an explicit grandfather-vs-withdraw pass (no silent inventory rips).

decisions.a_avail3_cutoff: b — Kayson teaches the full lawful Avail 1-3 set now; q55 components + §5.1 difficulty gate Avail-3 economically; Drop G adds R/X legality enforcement only
decisions.b_demolitions: a — demolitions (already a registered Technical skill in data/skills.yaml) is the skill_required for explosive schematics; zero engine cost, R&E-faithful. Drop D unblocked.

decisions.2_output_types: a — three new types: armor, explosive, gear (survival_gear folds into gear)
decisions.3_contraband: a — Avail-4/X outlaw-tech: black-market/faction/quest-learned only, never lawful trainers
decisions.5_worked_sample: yes — Gundark §10 rubric signed off; per-category drops mass-apply it
decisions.6_dangling_keys: a — WEG-stat stun_pistol + blaster_carbine into weapons.yaml
decisions.7_ui8_abi: yes — crafting_state ABI per design pass §3.4 accepted
decisions.8_commissary: a — future mode:'vendor' slot in the same shop_state message (UI-4b.commissary_vendor_merge resolved)

design_calls_resolved_recent.brian_mandate_mechanical_use: EVERY craftable item must have a DEFINED GAMEPLAY USE — a set of handcuffs imposes mechanical restrictions; a force detector alerts around Force use; etc. Acknowledged as potentially long-pole. Codified in crafting_integration_design_pass_v1.md §3.2a as the mechanical-use mandate: an item enters the craftable set only in the same drop as (or after) its mechanical consumer; items whose hook is itself a system (restraints, Force detection) are tiered long-pole and get their own design before content ships. This is the no-phantom/faucets-and-sinks invariant applied to items.

design_calls_resolved_recent.title: Equipment-reader straggler sweep (suite-triage drop 3)
design_calls_resolved_recent.resolution: 2026-06-10 full-suite run: 6 failures. Root cause 1: sheet_renderer.py equip_data NameError crashed +sheet (5 smokes). Symbol-level audit then found 12 more un-migrated equipment readers/writers (attack ignored equipped weapon; combat wear never applied; experiment always 'no weapon'; sell-equipped always 'nothing equipped'; vendor-droid stocking + every crafting fumble wiped worn armor; look/+weapons/+armor display lines vanished; force disarm left shape-2 weapons). All 13 sites migrated to read_equipment/equipment_keys/write_equipment with armor preservation. parse_equipment_json/serialize_equipment docstrings now LEGACY-flagged; tests/test_equipment_reader_stragglers.py source-pins zero live callers. Root cause 2: manifest golden room count stale 280 vs 287 (Lane D +7 Geonosis rooms) — bumped with provenance comment. Lesson re-confirmed: handoff 'all migrated' claims are unreliable without symbol-level grep of HEAD.

design_calls_resolved_recent.decision: Lane E3 SPLIT like E2: shipped the d66-table content half, DEFERRED the venue front_owner/true_owner split. (1) d66 Cantina Encounter Table (Wretched Hive 2C, 36 entries) delivered era-translated + B3/Q1-clean as engine/cantina_encounters.py, consumed by a BUILDER-gated +cantina GM scene-seeding command (full table, incl. the disruptive plot hooks fired on purpose) AND by an enrichment of the passive legacy `cantina` ambient pool (atmospheric subset only; disruptive beats kept out). (2) Owner-split DEFERRED as anti-phantom: HEAD has no investigation/territory consumer for a hidden owner (housing has a single shopfront_owner_id, no venue model), so the flag-pair would be schema-ahead-of-consumer; it ships with the mechanic that reads it. The 2A 7-field venue card and 2B 10-step framework remain captured in the extraction as the field set for a future venue generator (optional depth). Net: Lane E content complete through E3; owner-split is the one remaining E3 item, gated on its consumer.

design_calls_resolved_recent.ref: Part V / Drop 4 — social-Force design decisions (LOCKED)
design_calls_resolved_recent.decision: Locked decision set driving Drop 4a. (a) Mind-trick SPLIT: light suggestion = contested skill check, NO DSP; coercion/domination stays dark (DSP). engine/force_powers.py affect_mind is currently dark_side=True and must be split. (b) Influence resolution: PC target -> OFFERED EFFECT (target player RPs the outcome, never auto-override); NPC target -> engine resolves deterministically. KEY FINDING: affect_mind, telekinesis, life_sense, sense_force are NARRATIVE-ONLY at HEAD (print a margin/flavor line, change zero game state; affect_mind merely taxes a DSP for nothing). Self/combat powers (accelerate_healing, control_pain, remain_conscious, injure_kill) DO have teeth. III.1's real job = give the social/sense powers MECHANICAL HOOKS: affect_mind -> time-boxed vendor-price / guard-alarm / pry-info; sense-lie -> reveal hidden NPC flag; telekinesis -> real disarm/shove; danger-sense -> initiative reroll; telepathy -> Master/Padawan bond state (db has create_bond / get_active_bond_for_padawan / training_log); life_sense -> real being list. (c) Persistent threat = roaming high-DSP bounty hunter: DETERMINISTIC, NO new AI cost (extends engine/bounty_board.py + parser/pc_bounty_commands.py; the auto-DSP-bounty trigger itself is net-new but small). Status/prestige rewards only. Faction-AGNOSTIC by behaviour (targets DSP, not faction). CIS-droid & Hutt-enforcement REJECTED (both are PLAYER factions; CIS~=Geonosian). Optional flavor via the existing idle-Ollama queue, NEVER Haiku (Haiku reserved for Director). The communal 'rally' objective (other half of III.3) needs a NON-player-faction villain (dark cult / creature / rogue droids) -> decide at 4b. AI re-audit deferred to after the Director is fully built. (d) Memorial = marker / news only, no mechanical legacy. (e) Milestone-CP = NOT capped; meter-only in @economy. (g) Force visions = ship SIMPLE now; rich Director/G5 version tracked. force_sensitive stays a DERIVED state (never a save_character kwarg); all out-of-combat rolls via perform_skill_check. Build order: Drop 4a (mind-trick split + teeth on existing powers: affect_mind vendor/guard/info, TK disarm, life_sense/sense_force real info) -> Drop 4a.2 (new powers: telepathy bond, farseeing/danger-sense + simple vision, sense-lie + offered-effect consent path) -> 4b (III.3 communal half) -> G4 -> Drop 5 tail -> enrichment Lane A.

Proactive credit-velocity alerts shipped (economy audit #17 + R1 'velocity alerts' clause). Hourly credit_velocity_alert_tick evaluates server-wide net 1h flow against bands and pages online staff + records to @economy alerts. Bands are BIDIRECTIONAL (alert on inflation net>>0 AND deflation net<<0 — economy_audit_v2 flagged that farming detection's positive-delta-only watch lets a wage-heavy server deflate silently). INITIAL DEFAULT thresholds, NOT yet calibrated: VELOCITY_CAUTION_NET_1H=50,000, VELOCITY_CRITICAL_NET_1H=150,000 cr/hr (engine/economy_alerts.py, module-level for easy tuning). Open: Brian to calibrate against live @economy velocity data; a possible refinement is spike-vs-24h-baseline detection rather than a fixed band. Logic is server-free in engine/economy_alerts.py (unit-testable); the tick does all I/O and fails open.

Economy audit F1/R1 ('connect the ledger') COMPLETED. The Drop 1 line-grep missed multi-line calls; an AST scan found 14 save_character(credits=...) sites still bypassing the adjust_credits chokepoint (so their credit flow never reached credit_log and was invisible to @economy velocity + the velocity alerts). Migrated the 13 running-economy transactions to adjust_credits (preserving existing affordability pre-checks, allow_negative=True default): engine/organizations.py (org_stipend), engine/encounter_boarding.py (boarding_reward), engine/debt.py (debt_payment), parser/entertainer_commands.py x2 (entertainer), parser/medical_commands.py x2 (medical patient->healer legs), parser/sabacc_commands.py (sabacc), parser/spacer_quest_commands.py x2 (debt_payment), parser/builtin_commands.py (repair, item_sale), parser/combat_commands.py (bounty), and server/tick_handlers_economy.docking_fee_tick (docking_fee; also dropped its non-atomic save_character+log_credit two-step). ONE site allowlisted: server/api.py skip-starter-kit (chargen absolute-init, consistent with the unlogged create_character INSERT). New guard tests/test_ledger_chokepoint_complete.py AST-scans engine/parser/server and fails on any raw save_character(credits=) outside the allowlist (anti-regression lock). engine/chain_rewards.py docstring corrected (it already used adjust_credits). Sandbox: 146 passed across ledger/economy/combat/debt-gate suites + 5 guard tests, no regression. NOTE: entertainer/sabacc/medical/spacer-quest have no dedicated sandbox tests — rely on Brian's full suite + smoke.

Economy audit #5 (recurring docking fee) confirmed ALREADY IMPLEMENTED — scorecard was stale. server/tick_handlers_economy.docking_fee_tick charges 25 cr/day per docked player-owned ship (daily tick, interval=86400 offset=21600, 'economy hardening v23'). This drop also made it ledger-correct (migrated to adjust_credits, allow_negative=False so a broke owner is refused and keeps parking free as before).

Economy audit #10 (survey cooldown value mismatch) RESOLVED — Brian chose the audit's recommended value. engine/cooldowns.py SURVEY_COOLDOWN_S raised 300 -> 900 (5 -> 15 minutes). Rationale: free surveyed crafting materials should feel earned; the survey cooldown is the input throttle on the survey->craft->NPC-sell faucet flagged in economy_audit_v2 (5 min = 12 free pulls/hr feeding it; 15 min = 4). Partial mitigation only — the NPC buyback margin (Phase-3 #12) is the larger lever, deferred. tests/test_cooldowns_unit.py value-pin updated (test_survey_duration_is_5_minutes -> _15_minutes, asserts 900). test_economy_validation range check (>=120) still passes. 900s is the CANONICAL value (arch doc sw_d6_mush_architecture_v51.md lives outside the repo tree; add the line there if a doc record is wanted).

Fixed 2 of the 3 known pre-existing test failures. (1) tests/test_make_register_manifest.py: the local _frac_to_world helper still inverted y (y_max - fy*h); tool's world_to_frac is no-flip per Drop 4.17 (fy=(wy-y_min)/dy, SVG y-down) and renderer paints substrate at y_min. Fixed helper to y_min + fy*h; 6/6 pass. (2) tests/test_area_map_emits_slug.py: replaced deprecated asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete with the canonical fresh-loop pattern (new_event_loop + try/finally close) per BugFix5; 3/3 pass, no deprecation. Broader get_event_loop() pattern (~16 sites) left — shielded by pytest-asyncio managed loop, sweeping risks churn.

Re-pins: (1) terrain_zones substrate-aware (painted region w/ substrate_image needs no procedural zones); (2) manifest test -> builder fidelity (manifest converts every authored landmark to an in-bounds pin, exits not distinctive) replacing the fixed 6/4 that was wrong for both regions (Dune Sea 5, Underworld 6 -- both curated/complete, nothing dropped); (3) old package {GEOGRAPHY} franchise-ban (lines moved to generated briefs in Drop 4.18) -> test_package_documents_generated_briefs (package references gen_wilderness_overview.py + the brief paths, which exist); (4) JS substrate check repointed from body-renderer commented-placeholder to ACTIVE substrate_image in m3_wilderness_overview_data.js (body confirmed data-driven). Economy audit v2 §1.3 (NPC vendor buyback price-supports crafting) RESOLVED via the audit's PREFERRED indirect approach: NPC vendors refuse well-made player crafts, pushing them to the player vendor-droid market. New engine/items.py CRAFTED_NPC_BUYBACK_MAX_QUALITY=50 (tunable) + npc_refuses_buyback(item) helper (True iff item.crafter set AND quality>=threshold). parser/builtin_commands.py SellCommand gated by it before pricing/credit write; low-quality crafts (<50) and all factory items (new_from_vendor, no crafter) still sell as salvage. Closes the craft->NPC-sell money-printer (was >250 cr/craft) and removes the NPC price-floor that suppressed the player crafted-goods market. The pre-existing sell-price quality bonus (>=80 +30%, >=60 +15%) is now unreachable for crafted items (gated at >=50) and irrelevant for factory items (quality 50); left in place (harmless). DISTINCT from #12 (dynamic supply/demand curves), still deferred Month-1+. Tests: tests/test_npc_buyback_crafted.py (+6); 152 passed across sell/craft/weapon suites, no regression. Optional future tightening (audit 'direct' option): a 10-15% salvage rate for the crafted items NPCs DO still buy.

Lane C (Gundark's gear/crafting expansion) gate resolved as OPTION B — SPLIT by faucet/sink (Brian: 'Split the faucet/sink'). The purchasable/vendor gear families (a pure anti-inflationary credit SINK, balance-gated by Gundark's §8 availability cheat-sheet + WEG D6 re-stats, NOT by farming controls) may land NOW, shipped in waves by family. The craftable/lootable gear families (a value/income FAUCET) stay gated behind Drop-5 farming controls per the standing faucets-and-sinks-land-together rule. Grep basis: Drop-3 sinks effectively satisfied (A-items complete; 3c optional); faucet bounds already present (creature_spoils tier-cap, SURVEY_COOLDOWN_S=900, trading.py per-round-trip ceiling); the missing prerequisite is the GENERAL Drop-5 farming layer, which gates only the faucet wave.

Markets are LOCAL by design — NO galaxy-wide auction house (correcting a prior misframe). Posture is discovery-not-marketplace / EVE-regional-lite: a read-only, planet-scoped listing index + buy orders, NO remote execution; the WoW-style global AH is explicitly rejected for a 5–50 pop game. Already partially realized in HEAD: parser/shop_commands.py MarketSearchCommand ('market search [planet|all]') lists planet-scoped player shopfronts via engine.housing.get_market_directory, and 'market <planet>' shows cargo prices. The only outstanding Drop-5 '+market' work is ITEM-LEVEL discovery + a buy-order index layered on that existing planet-scoped directory — not a new marketplace, not galaxy-wide.

The Lane D E'Y-Akh POIs (marmio_mio_freighter, ng_zi_badlands) deliberately carry NO ambient_lines and put all flavor in `description` (the look-time path) to avoid this dead seam; tests/test_lane_d_ey_akh_landmarks.py pins that. tech_debt.name: consumable_storage_unification
tech_debt.summary: Two parallel storage models exist for consumables. (1) attributes.consumables = {key: count} — used by stims/medpacs, written by crafting layer for output_type: consumable, consumed by stim command. (2) inventory.items = [...] with consumable:true flag — used by bacta packs etc., handled by UseCommand.
tech_debt.consequence: UseCommand cannot operate on stims; stim command cannot operate on bacta packs. Future consumable types must pick a model arbitrarily. Crafting output is bifurcated based on schematic output_type alone, not on category.
tech_debt.remediation_outline: Migrate attributes.consumables entries into inventory.items as {type: consumable, key, quality, count} (stacked by key), or alternatively as individual unstacked items. Update add_to_inventory to handle stacking by key. Update parser/crafting_commands.py::consumable branch to write to inventory. Update parser/medical_commands.py::has_consumable + consume_consumable to read/write inventory. Extend UseCommand to handle stims (which would obsolete much of the stim-command flow but the offer/accept ritual is still wanted for cross-character application). Schema migration required for live characters.
tech_debt.effort_estimate: Medium (~2 sess) — data migration + consumer migration + likely DB upgrade.
tech_debt.trigger_to_fix: Add a third consumable category (anything that isn't a stim, medpac, or bacta pack) — at that point the third author is forced to pick a model and the bifurcation pain is loudest.

tech_debt.why_not_fixed_here: The fix is a map-layout/cardinal-correctness DESIGN call, not a mechanical one, and the authored signals conflict: room 41's coords (7,3) place it due-WEST of the gate (8,3), but its description says 'Outside the city wall' (desert-side = east), and the checkpoint (44) is ALSO east per its text. Grid-adjacency suggests the gate's existing direct 'west'->room 8 (city, x=5) should instead hit the adjacent room 41 (x=7). Multiple valid resolutions, no derivable ground truth — Brian's per-map domain (cf. the May 29-30 relayout philosophies A/B). Not guessed at.
remediation_options: Option A (re-coord, matches description): move room 41 to a free desert-side tile (e.g. (9,4) SE of the gate; 44 holds (9,3)) and set the 40<->41 exit to southeast/northwest so coords + direction + description agree. Touches map coords -> check it against any relaid outskirts map.

Option B (ordinal deconfliction, keep coords): keep 40->8 'west' and 40->44 'east'; give 40<->41 a free ordinal (e.g. gate 'southeast'->market, market 'northwest'->gate). Resolves the collision but worsens cardinal-vs-coord agreement (coords say due-west), which runs against the relayout goal.

Option C (re-route the west corridor): make the gate's 'west' go to the ADJACENT scavenger market (41) and have the market continue 'west' to the city (8), instead of the gate jumping straight to room 8. Changes player navigation topology.
tech_debt.verification: After whichever fix: `python -c "from engine.world_loader import load_world_dry_run as L; print(L('clone_wars').report.ok)"` must print True, and tests/test_f1d_era_switch.py (esp. test_cw_no_test_character, which then asserts exactly 1 built character) must pass.
tech_debt.effort_estimate: Small once the layout choice is made (1-3 line YAML edit, or a coord move + exit edit).
tech_debt.trigger_to_fix: Before launch (fresh installs need a buildable world) — or whenever the outskirts cardinal pass happens.
tech_debt.resolution: Option A (Brian's call). data/worlds/clone_wars/planets/tatooine.yaml: moved room 41 (scavenger_market) from (7,3) to desert-side (9,4) SE of the gate, and re-pointed the 40<->41 edge to SE/NW (was the inverted 'west'/'east to Scavenger Market'). This frees room 40's duplicate 'east' slot for the checkpoint (44) and also corrects the 43->41 'east' forward. VERIFIED in sandbox: load_world_dry_run('clone_wars').report.ok flipped False->True, errors 1->0, warnings unchanged at 29 (no regressions; outskirts edges now clean). Final gate: Brian's full pytest on Windows (test_f1d_era_switch::test_cw_no_test_character).

tech_debt.name: m3_click_to_walk_cannot_leave_via_non_marker_street_exits
tech_debt.added_by: Brian's 2026-05-31 browser smoke-test of the substrate lane (Mos Eisley district modal)
tech_debt.priority: HIGH (launch UX) — a player can get funneled into a dead-end room pair with no clickable way out.
tech_debt.symptom: In the TIER-1 DISTRICT modal (Mos Eisley substrate), standing in Bay 94 Entrance (server room 0) the player can only click down->pit(1) and back up; cannot reach the adjacent Tower or anywhere else.
tech_debt.diagnosis: Root cause is certain from the graph + the decoration code. Bay 94 Entrance (room 0) has exactly TWO exits: down->pit(1) and north->spaceport-street-hub(7). Everything else on that map (Tower=26, Customs, Speeders, bays) hangs off the hub (room 7). The M3 click-to-walk decoration (static/client.html _buildAdjacencyByRenderId, ~L5770-5875) attaches class=rm-adj + data-travel-dir only to elements carrying data-room-id; the delegated handler reads data-travel-dir and calls sendCmd(dir). The pit is a clickable marker so down/up work, but the one lateral exit out (north->7) leads to a STREET hub. NOTE: under a substrate L_SubstrateRooms DOES emit data-room-id for ALL rooms incl. room 7 (m3_composition_engine ~L255), so the failure is one of: (a) the District/POI render branch in the screenshot is NOT the L_SubstrateRooms path (POI overlay only marks landmark/building rooms), or (b) adjacency resolution (name-match then spatial fallback) fails to bind 'north' to room 7. There is also no pathfinding, so distant POIs (Tower) are not clickable regardless. Multiple render branches; not runtime-verifiable in the AST sandbox -> rendering-layer-phantom risk -> NOT blind-patched.
tech_debt.verification_probe: 30-sec browser console check in the open District modal: (1) document.querySelectorAll('[data-room-id]').length and whether room 7's render-id appears; (2) inspect the adjacency map for the player's room to see if 'north'->7 is present. Output disambiguates sub-case (a) vs (b).
remediation_options: PREFERRED — view-independent directional exit HUD: render the current room's non-hidden exits as clickable direction controls in the modal chrome (wired to sendCmd(dir)). Always works regardless of which render branch is active or whether the destination has a marker. Best web-first nav.

Targeted — if the probe shows room 7 HAS a data-room-id cell but isn't decorated, fix adjacency resolution so non-building/street destinations get rm-adj + data-travel-dir. Smaller, reuses the proven path.
Richer follow-up — pathfinding click-to-walk: clicking any room runs BFS over the exit graph and walks the route (issues the first step). Makes distant POIs (Tower) directly clickable. Larger change.
tech_debt.effort_estimate: small-to-medium once the probe picks the sub-case; HUD is the safe default.
tech_debt.trigger_to_fix: Before launch (navigation is core); ship the patch on Brian's go after the probe.
tech_debt.status: fix shipped (v2) — pending browser smoke-test (hard-refresh first)
tech_debt.probe_result: CONFIRMED sub-case (a). The District-modal render branch does NOT emit data-room-id for street/hub rooms: ids 6,7,8,9,10,11,47 (all streets, incl. the spaceport hub room 7) are absent from document.querySelectorAll('[data-room-id]'); and only ['down','down'] was decorated (doubled = two render passes). So the lone lateral exit out of Bay 94 Entrance (north->hub 7) had no clickable target.
tech_debt.resolution: Added an EXIT STRIP to the SECTOR MAP modal (static/client.html): a new #map-modal-exits row, built from the cached current-room exits (lastExits = [{dir,label}]), rendering one clickable button per non-hidden exit regardless of whether the destination has a map marker. Each button issues sendCmd(dir) then closeMapModal() — mirroring the existing marker travel handler (drops the player into the live view in the new room). Edits: HTML container, CSS (.map-modal-exits/.mm-exit-btn, themed to the chrome), clear-on-open, populate in the ground branch, and the _renderModalExitStrip() fn. node --check clean on the inline script block. Once the player steps to the hub via the strip, the surrounding building markers are already click-to-walkable, so full navigation is restored without pathfinding. NOT yet browser-verified (rendering-layer-phantom discipline) — needs Brian's Windows smoke-test.
tech_debt.v1_ordering_bug: v1 of the exit strip shipped a runtime ordering bug: _renderModalExitStrip() is called from openMapModal()'s ground branch (~L8189) BEFORE mapModalOpen is set true (~L8202), but the fn guarded on `if (!mapModalOpen) return;` — so it bailed every open and the strip never populated (then :empty hid it). node --check passed (valid syntax, dead at runtime). FIXED in v2: removed the bogus guard. Lesson: trace the call-order, not just syntax, for UI hooks the sandbox can't run.

tech_debt.name: post_move_bearing_update_NameError_every_move
tech_debt.priority: MEDIUM — fired on EVERY move; bearing/facing chevron never updated. Move itself succeeded (caught warning).
tech_debt.summary: parser/builtin_commands.py _post_move_hooks line ~1444 referenced `exit_data` and `direction`, neither of which were parameters of the hook (signature was (self, ctx, session, char, new_room_id)). NameError on exit_data -> 'MoveCommand: bearing update failed' on every move.

tech_debt.name: jedi_order_hq_room_name_points_at_nonexistent_room
tech_debt.priority: LOW-MEDIUM — latent. Does NOT affect intel handover (that's room-based on the player's location, and the Jedi handler is tagged on Knight Halen Voss in the real Entrance Hall room). But any system resolving the Jedi HQ by name (housing HQ establishment via organizations.hq_room_id) would fail to find the room.
tech_debt.detail: organizations.yaml line ~141 sets jedi_order hq_room_name = 'Coruscant - Jedi Temple Main Hall'. No room with that name exists — the real Temple rooms are 'Jedi Temple - Main Gate', '- Entrance Hall', '- Training Hall', '- Archives', '- Council Antechamber', '- High Council Chamber', '- Meditation Garden', '- Medical Bay', '- Hangar Bay', '- Commissary'. The string 'Jedi Temple Main Hall' appears ONLY at organizations.yaml:141 — nothing else references it (grep-confirmed), so changing it is safe.
tech_debt.recommended_fix: Reconcile hq_room_name to a real room — e.g. 'Jedi Temple - Entrance Hall' (aligns with the intel handler now placed there) or 'Jedi Temple - High Council Chamber' (more HQ-like, but restricted). NOT changed in this drop: hq_room_name feeds housing HQ resolution, a separate concern from intel handlers, and the change can't be runtime-verified in the AST sandbox. Flagged for Brian's call (one-line edit).
tech_debt.resolution: Drop 2026-06-05: organizations.yaml jedi_order hq_room_name set to 'Jedi Temple - Entrance Hall' (a real room in planets/coruscant.yaml). Brian decision 1 = Entrance Hall. Verified by tests/test_t2def_covert_handlers.py.

tech_debt.name: ship_ownership_not_enforced_in_control_path
tech_debt.summary: ships.owner_id is set on every ship (brokerage, spacer-quest, builder spawn) but is NOT consulted by BoardCommand or PilotCommand. board: no owner check. pilot: only checks seat occupancy. launch: requires pilot, but the pilot seat is grabbable by anyone aboard. Net: any player can board any docked ship and fly it.
tech_debt.consequence: Theft/grief of player-owned ships; 3a worsens it by parking valuable owned hulls at a public landing pad. No crew-authorization concept exists (faction issued_ships is a separate table, not consulted here).
tech_debt.remediation_outline: Add _is_authorized_for_ship(char_id, ship) -> owner_id match OR membership in an 'authorized' allow-list stored in crew JSON. Gate PilotCommand (and thus launch) on it. Keep boarding open by default (RP) unless Brian wants it gated. Add +ship crew/permit/revoke command for the owner to authorize co-pilots. Pending Brian's model choice (see design_calls_pending_brian T2.ECON.ship_ownership_gate).
tech_debt.resolution: PilotCommand + LaunchCommand now gate on engine/ship_access.is_authorized_pilot (owner OR authorized_pilots allow-list in systems JSON; unowned fails open; admin bypass). +shipcrew lets the owner manage the roster. Boarding intentionally left open.

TD.DIRECTOR_FACTION_MODEL_GCW: The Director's INTERNAL faction model is still GCW-keyed (deferred from E1 2026-06-04 per Brian's option A; E1 cleaned only the player-facing narrative layer + repaired the milestone feature). Surfaces, all engine/director.py: (1) ZoneState has hardcoded fields imperial/rebel/criminal/independent (defaults 50/10/50/30) and get_faction(f)=getattr(self,f,0) — get_faction('republic') is 0 unless zone-init set_faction'd it (it does at ~361-370, so the milestone AVERAGES work, but the static fields are stale GCW); (2) compile_digest() (~495) reports zs.imperial/rebel/criminal/independent to the LLM in the Faction Turn payload — the LLM gets GCW faction influence, NOT the live CW factions; (3) AlertLevel.compute_alert (~191) thresholds key on self.imperial/self.rebel; (4) ActionDigest default kills_by_faction/faction_talks dicts (~221/229) seed GCW keys (reset() uses VALID_FACTIONS so they self-correct after one cycle); (5) influence-adjustment special-cases (~454-479) reference criminal/imperial (dead in CW — adj validation rejects non-VALID_FACTIONS first); (6) VALID_FACTION_CODES (~943) = {empire,rebel,hutt,bh_guild} gates the LLM's faction_orders -> CW faction orders are rejected. LLM-context/internal only (NO player-facing string leak). Requires a GCW->CW faction-mapping decision. Larger drop. NOTE: the emergency-fallback baselines (~83-104) MUST stay GCW-byte-equivalent (flag-off legacy path; pinned by test_f6a3_int_byte_equivalence) — do NOT touch them.

TD.HUTT_AUCTION_Q1_JABBA: STRING RESOLVED 2026-06-04 (E2). The live player-facing 'Jabba' named-figure leaks are cleaned -> world_events.py HUTT_AUCTION announce_text ('A Hutt kajidic is running an auction') and _ZONE_DISPLAY_NAMES['jabba'] ('the Hutt quarter'). TD.PATROL_SPAWN_MULT_PHANTOM_REPAIRED: RESOLVED 2026-06-04 (E2). PHANTOM FINDING that overturns the 'patrol_spawn_mult already-live' claim (memory/TODO/E1 EVENT_DEFS note): engine/npc_space_traffic.py had TWO `_pick_archetype` defs — the first (world-event-aware, read patrol_spawn_mult) was SHADOWED by a later `_pick_archetype(exclude_hunter=True)` (the one actually called by _spawn), so patrol_spawn_mult had NEVER been consumed at runtime. No test covered it (test_e1 only asserts the effect is DECLARED on the event, not consumed). FIX: deleted the dead duplicate; consolidated BOTH multipliers (patrol + pirate, each max(0,int(weight*mult)), with a total<=0 -> TRADER guard) into the LIVE def. Now both are genuinely consumed (proved by consumption tests: patrol share 0.158->0.298 under SECURITY_CRACKDOWN; pirate 0.173->0.378 under PIRATE_SURGE). Regression guard: tests/test_e2_dormant_effects.py::test_only_one_pick_archetype_definition pins exactly one def.

TD.DIRECTOR_ACTIVATE_EVENT_SIGNATURE_MISMATCH: RESOLVED 2026-06-06 (Director-dispatch fix drop, same-session rollup). Rewrote BOTH broken call sites in engine/director.py to the real sync activate_event(evt_type, zones=, duration_minutes=, headline=) and added the await _broadcast_activation(activated, session_mgr) the timer path uses, so Director-fired narrative events now activate AND announce; the era-milestone path's non-existent create_event() was converted to activate_event(zones=list(VALID_ZONES)). 5 tests in tests/test_director_event_dispatch.py prove the call contract + a real broadcast + structural pins; the full Faction Turn integration is Windows-gated (AI-provider/DB harness). ORIGINAL FINDING (Lane E2a): engine/director.py (~857) calls `await wem.activate_event(db, session_mgr, event_type=evt_type, zones_affected=zones, duration_minutes=duration, headline=headline, source='director')`, but the ONLY activate_event (engine/world_events.py:436) is SYNC with signature `(event_type, zones=None, duration_minutes=None, headline='')` — no db/session_mgr params, no `source`/`zones_affected` kwargs, not awaitable. The call is a guaranteed TypeError, so EVERY Director-fired narrative_event is currently dead (masked: it's inside the Director response handler, which the LLM path may rarely reach, and any raise is logged not fatal). World events still fire via the WORKING timer path (tick -> activate_event(etype.value) -> _broadcast_activation). CONSEQUENCE: the sandwhirl's 'use-the-terrain' Director-narrated catch is phantom until fixed; the storms' graded penalties are real via the timer. FIX (own focused drop + test): rewrite the call to the real signature `ev = wem.activate_event(evt_type, zones, duration, headline); if ev: await wem._broadcast_activation(ev, session_mgr)` (drop db/session_mgr/source positionals/kwargs), then verify the room-state SQL + the effect_text broadcast bug below don't surface once the path is live. Turning on a long-dead subsystem is deliberately out of an atomic storm drop's scope.

TD.WORLD_EVENT_EFFECT_TEXT_MISSING: RESOLVED 2026-06-06 (Director-dispatch fix drop). Added effect_text: str = '' as a trailing defaulted EventDef field (all instantiations use kwargs, so the default applies safely) and populated it for the three storms; _broadcast_activation now sends the web 'effects' summary on both the timer and Director paths (proven by test_broadcast_activation_sends_web_effects_without_swallowing). ORIGINAL FINDING (Lane E2a): engine/world_events.py _broadcast_activation (~527) references `edef.effect_text` in the web-client structured payload, but EventDef has NO effect_text field (fields: event_type/name/announce_text/expire_text/default_duration_min/max/timer_probability/preferred_zones/mechanical_effects) -> AttributeError, swallowed by the surrounding `except Exception: pass`. EFFECT: the web client's `world_event` 'effects' string silently never sends; the Telnet broadcast (session_mgr.broadcast of announce_text) works, and the 'start'/'end' web banners still send minus the effects field. FIX (one-liner): either add an `effect_text: str = ''` default field to EventDef and author per-event, or send a short summary derived from mechanical_effects, or drop the key. Low-risk but a behavior change (the web client would start receiving effects text), so kept out of the storm drop.

TD.WORLD_EVENT_ZONE_MODEL: FLAGGED 2026-06-06 (Lane E2a; pre-existing, extends the TD.HUTT_AUCTION_Q1_JABBA note). get_effect() is GLOBAL (any active event's effect applies everywhere), and preferred_zones uses an abstract taxonomy (spaceport/streets/cantina/jabba/government) not the real CW zone keys (tatooine_*). CONSEQUENCE for E2a: a sandstorm/gravel/sandwhirl active anywhere penalizes perception AND ranged combat for ALL players on ALL planets, including indoors and on non-desert worlds (a sandwhirl could narratively fire on Kamino/Coruscant). This is the SAME coarseness the live SANDSTORM perception_penalty already has (accepted since 2026-06-04) — E2a matches that precedent rather than fixing it. FIX (separate, larger): zone-scope the storms (real CW zone keys + planet gating) and have the consumers read get_effects_for_zone(player_zone) keyed on the player's/ship's actual room->zone->planet, gated to outdoor/desert. Combat would need room props threaded into CombatInstance at creation (the resolver is sync, no db). Belongs with the planet/zone-resolution work, not a storm content drop.

tech_debt.name: test_clickwalk_slugjoin x4 fail on Windows but pass in Linux sandbox
tech_debt.summary: tests/spa/test_clickwalk_slugjoin.py x4 PASS in the Linux sandbox (node v22.22.2: 6 passed, 1 skipped) and already skip when node is absent, but FAIL on Brian's Windows box (node present). Environmental — likely node-version or Windows subprocess/newline/temp-file quirk. No E3 code change.
tech_debt.remediation_outline: Capture proc.stderr from the node subprocess on Brian's box (the test shells out to node); compare node version; check for CRLF / temp-file path handling in the harness.
tech_debt.trigger_to_fix: When Brian can paste the node stderr from his box.
tech_debt.status: RESOLVED 2026-06-06 (Drop A hygiene) - root cause found without the stderr: the harness wrote its node script via NamedTemporaryFile('w') and decoded node stdout via subprocess.run(text=True), both with NO encoding -> cp1252 on Windows. The injected SPA module source contains non-ASCII (em/en-dashes, multiplication sign), so cp1252 corrupted the .mjs on write AND mangled captured stdout. Both vectors forced to encoding='utf-8'. Same class as C1 (m3_tokens WOUNDED x2 label) and B2 (inline-script check); swept across all 6 SPA node harnesses. Pinned by tests/test_drop_a_hygiene_encoding.py.

tech_debt.name: Character object holds equipped_weapon/worn_armor as bare key strings, not ItemInstances

phantom_pattern_catalog_summary.name: TODO drift (P2 sub-pattern)
phantom_pattern_catalog_summary.summary: v48-NEW (May 24 combined drop). Items resolved-at-HEAD but tracked-as-pending in TODO.json because the resolving drop didn't update the tracker. Remediation: every feature-closing drop updates TODO.json status in the same drop.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: extend_dont_add
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: Before creating a new command/file/module, check whether an existing surface already does something semantically adjacent. If yes, extend it as a subform/option/output addition.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: pre_apply_steps_must_be_section
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: Drops that retire files MUST have a 'Pre-apply step' section in the handoff calling out the deletion explicitly.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: force_sensitive_is_derived
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: force_sensitive is derived from Force-attribute keys in attributes JSON. NOT a column. Never pass as save_character() kwarg.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: smoke_uses_slug_lookup
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: Smoke scenarios must use room_id_by_slug(), never hardcoded DB ids.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: drain_text_needs_ever_nonempty
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: _drain_text ever_nonempty flag must be present - buffer must receive content before declaring quiet.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: tracker_update_in_same_drop
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: Every drop that ships a feature closing a TODO.json item updates that item's status field in the same drop. Same principle as extend-don't-add: tracking discipline matches code-shipping discipline. Prevents P2.a TODO drift.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: minimal_substrate_first
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: Large systems ship in waves where the first wave provides the substrate (schema, selector, integration hook, content) and observable behavior is minimal (a player-visible message). Dispatch logic (NPC spawn, vendor wiring, weather effects) lands in follow-up sub-drops. Same principle that brought the wilderness regions online in T2.3.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: design_doc_locks_before_drop_sequences
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: Multi-drop pivots (>3 drops in a planned sequence) get a locked design doc BEFORE the first drop ships. The doc names the drop sequence (SYN.0-SYN.N), captures decisions resolved during step-back conversations, and becomes the source of truth that each subsequent drop's handoff references. Mid-sequence design changes require an explicit v.N+1 revision with supersede note; silent drift is a Phantom-1/Phantom-2 violation.

named_disciplines_quick_ref.name: competitive_review_for_genre_features
named_disciplines_quick_ref.rule: When introducing features that have established genre conventions (territory warfare, crafting economies, world bosses), consult the relevant inspirations explicitly before locking design. The competitive review for the May 24 pivot pulled lessons from EVE/Albion/SWG/Foxhole/Eco/RotMG and changed three core knobs (contest centrality, culminating fight, harvest model). Skipping this would have shipped a locally-coherent system that genre veterans would have immediately recognized as missing common patterns.

Combat sources weapon damage AND armor protection from the REGISTRY by key (attack helper combat_commands.py:~1635; get_armor_protection) — crafted quality and experiment stat boosts on the ItemInstance are never read by combat math. Quality today = condition/max_condition/value/experiment-base only. Convention-consistent across weapons+armor (Drop C matches weapons deliberately), but it means the economy's "better crafted gear" promise is decay/value-side, not roll-side, and EXPERIMENT BOOSTS MAY BE DISPLAY-ONLY. Related: TD.EQUIPMENT_CHARACTER_HOLDS_KEYS_NOT_INSTANCES. Needs a deliberate quality->combat-stats design pass (candidate for the crafting follow-up lane); flagged, not half-built.

investigation_notes: 2026-06-05 (Sourcebook Enrichment Lane A, Phase B — encounter->spawner bridge, T2.WENC.b, 'Do B. Roll up'): Wired the creature library into LIVE combat. Until now a hostile wilderness encounter fired narrative only; payload.npc_template was read nowhere at runtime. Shipped seam-first (mirrors the dsp_hunter spawn). NEW engine/creature_library.py (PURE): load_creature_library (cached), get_creature, resolve_natural_attack, build_creature_char_sheet, build_creature_ai_config, creature_spawn_count. THE faithfulness crux = resolve_natural_attack: 13/14 creatures carry WEG-relative prose damage ('STR+1D'/'STR+2D+2'/'STR+2'), absolute ('1D'), or specials ('Poison 5D', grapple 'opposed (..)', '+3D/round'). It parses the clean STR(+ND)(+N)/ND(+N) forms into a concrete dice string via DicePool (so pips normalize: magus STR1D+1 +'STR+2' -> 2D; stalker STR3D +'STR+2D+2' -> 5D+2; voroos STR6D +'STR+1D,then grasp' -> 7D + special flag) and FALLS BACK to bare STR for poison/grapple/multi-round riders while flagging special so the spawn flavor still carries the rider (full poison/grapple MECHANICS = documented follow-up). MINIMAL combat-path change so creatures fight faithfully not at bare STR: engine/character.py Character gains natural_attack_skill/damage fields set in from_npc_sheet from the char_sheet's natural_attack marker; engine/npc_combat_ai.py::_get_npc_weapon honors it FIRST (returns (brawling, resolved_damage, '')) before the equipped-weapon/bare-STR fallback — ordinary NPCs unaffected (fields default ''). NEW engine/wilderness_encounter_runtime.py (IO): spawn_encounter_creatures (loads creature, builds faithful sheet+ai, db.create_npc x count; count = payload.count else pack low else 1) + _start_encounter_combat (mirrors dsp_hunter_runtime._start_hunter_combat) + resolve_encounter_spawn orchestrator. Wired guarded at parser/builtin_commands.py (~1316) after the encounter narrative: hostile/non_hostile + resolvable npc_template -> spawn; hostile -> combat starts immediately. +18 tests (tests/test_lane_a_phase_b_spawner.py) against the REAL resolver/builders/Character/_get_npc_weapon + the bridge spawn-logic via a fake-db (asserts N creates with the faithful 5D+2 sheet, hostile flag, numbered names, and no-spawn for weather/anomaly/no-template/unknown). 18/18 green; existing 3 Lane A suites still green (additive); combat/character sandbox tests green. DEFERRED (documented): special-attack mechanics (poison/grapple/swallow beyond base damage); remaining biomes (Kamino/Geonosis still region-less; Kuat miner's_horror starfighter-scale -> space path); creature-harvest sinks (still no runtime path). PENDING Brian: full Windows suite + a live smoke (wander a region until a hostile creature encounter fires -> the creature SPAWNS and combat starts; verify e.g. a stalker lizard hits for ~5D+2, not 3D). Handoff: HANDOFF_lane_a_phase_b_spawner_2026-06-05.md.

[DELIVERED 2026-06-05] 2026-06-05 (Lane A Phase B / T2.WENC.b scoping, from a 'continue the rollups' investigation): Lane A Phase A (creature library + encounters + lore for both live wilderness regions) is DONE and on HEAD (data/npcs_creatures.yaml = 14 creatures; tests/test_lane_a_creatures_{tatooine,coruscant,crossbiome}.py = 24 green; already in the prior upload + passed the full Windows suite). Phase B = the encounter->spawner bridge: wire the hostile encounter's payload.npc_template to load faithful stats and spawn the creature(s) as hostile combatant(s). Call site is parser/builtin_commands.py:~1297 (narrative-only today, marked T2.WENC.b follow-up); payload.npc_template is read NOWHERE at runtime yet. Spawn pattern to mirror = engine/wilderness_anomalies.py ~2740 (db.create_npc + ai_cfg['hostile']=True). KEY FINDING that makes this a CORE-COMBAT drop, not a trivial wire: combat reads NPC attack via engine/npc_combat_ai.py::_get_npc_weapon -> registry weapon damage, else brawling + bare STR for unarmed. It does NOT read natural_attack. But 13/14 creatures have natural_attack.damage != STR (mostly WEG-relative 'STR+1D'/'STR+2D+2'/'STR+2', plus specials 'Poison 5D', grapple 'opposed (brawling vs..)', '+3D/round once it has the head'). So faithful damage needs (a) a pure resolve_natural_attack_damage() that parses the clean 'STR+ND[+N]'/'ND' forms (~11/14) and falls back to STR + special-flavor for the ~3 prose/grapple/poison forms, and (b) a small _get_npc_weapon enhancement to honor a creature natural-attack marker on the char_sheet/ai_config. Recommended shape (mirrors the dsp_hunter seam-first drop from this session): NEW engine/creature_library.py (load_creature_library cached + resolve_natural_attack_damage + build_creature_char_sheet + build_creature_ai_config — all PURE, fully unit-testable incl. the resolver across all 14 forms) + the _get_npc_weapon enhancement (unit-tested in isolation) + a spawn_encounter_creatures(db, entry, room_id) bridge (tested with _FakeDB asserting N creates with faithful sheets) + the guarded wire at the call site. Live spawn+combat = Windows gate (needs aiosqlite + the combat loop), same as the dsp_hunter spawn. Special-attack mechanics (poison/grapple/multi-round) = documented follow-up beyond base damage. DELIVERED seam-first (engine/creature_library.py + the Character/_get_npc_weapon natural-attack hook + engine/wilderness_encounter_runtime.py + the guarded builtin_commands wire + 18 tests). Live spawn+combat = Windows gate.

launch_criteria: CLARIFIED by Brian 2026-06-06: launch = nearly ALL of the enrichment roadmap (Lanes A-F) AND the remainder of the overall roadmap implemented BEFORE launch -- NOT a minimal viable cut. Rationale: avoid invasive post-launch changes (schema/state) that could break the live game. The whole backlog is therefore PRE-LAUNCH scope. Implication: T3.19 (tunables+telemetry), T3.20 (state-preservation robustness), T3.21 (optimization/security), and the arch-doc v52 reconciliation are ALL pre-launch hardening, clustered at the END after features are stable. The 'tier_3_post_launch' bucket name is now partly a misnomer -- most of it is pre-launch; only the genuinely-post-launch tuning LOOP (Claude analyzing telemetry to adjust config) stays post-launch.
implementation_discipline: Brian 2026-06-06: do NOT blindly implement TODO items as written. TODO captures intent + a first-draft of design-heavy items; at implementation time Claude must re-examine / rethink the design (the telemetry catalog under T3.19 is the named example, but this applies broadly). Pair this with the standing phantom-delivery discipline (symbol-grep HEAD before claiming anything; memory + handoffs are untrusted without verification).
